reviews.comment: The exams will test both your knowledge and your mental fortitude. However! The projects and weekly assignments are easy so long as you follow all instructions. Attend class or else. Still, he gives the answers to each in-class activity.

Sal is a nice guy but his lectures are torture. If you can manage to pay attention during these though the class is smooth sailing. Always found the complaints about needing to memorize the unix manual funny because if you pay attention he'll bring it up once every class, but most students in the section are busy playing games on their laptops.
Just don't. He literally asks which page some unix commands are found in the manual on the midterms/final. Extremely test heavy and his boring lectures don't help at all.

Genuinely not as bad as what the reviews say. Assigns a total of 12 labs and 4 projects which are time-consuming but doable provided you manage your time correctly. Lectures can get a bit dry but overall are fairly engaging and fairly paced. Is strict on phone usage but besides that is a genuinely good professor.
reviews.ratingTags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: draconian. why are we expected to know what page something is in the manual. that is what the manual is for.

I expected the worst having Sal as the only professor available. Turns out he's not a bad person at all. Lectures were comprehensive but excruciatingly boring because of his robotic pacing and the 80-min periods this semester. Weekly labs and regular projects make this class involving but manageable. Exams are very self-study-able using the slides.

I've never had a professor who wants to see his students fail as much as this dude. To give you an idea of what I mean, his midterm and final had questions asking for us to recall specific section / page numbers in the Unix User manual. And he was a crashout.

Long lectures with lots of tiny details. Do not skip class at all--frequent pop quizzes and assignments track your attendance. Several projects assigned, start them early as late penalties are harsh. The required "lab" period can be very useful if you do the work, but do not slack! Exams seem to test on obscure content rather than application.

Memorization heavy. Understand all powerpoints he posts, and you should be good for pop quizzes and midterm/final exams. Come to class every day for ALEs because you'll get a 0 otherwise. The 13 Labs and 4 projects are not too hard if you understand the concepts, but start early. Overall, just know you need to put in the work and stay consistent.
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: This is just a boring class, but attendance is mandatory. It is hard to want to pay attention in this class. It isn't a hard class to pass if you just put in the time. There are pop quizzes but it is on eLc and in class activities that count towards your grade, so it is important to show up. He isn't the kindest person and is very strict on phones.
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: He is not the best CS professor. The final grade depends entirely on two exams and no review questions are provided before the tests. Attendance is mandatory, and he will yell at students for using their phones during class. To do well, you need to learn about 90% of the material from the textbook and only 10% from the lectures.

The worst professor in the CS Department, extremely boring lectures and was always rude to students, the class has 13 active learning exercises, 10 quizzes, 13 labs, 4 projects and 2 exams worth 50% (25% each), he does not even drop the lowest grade in any of these sections stated above and is a very strict grader, Do not take him.
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Lectures were extremely dry, and are best described as robotic. Lamarca sounds like Mickey Mouse, except he doesn't care about his students whatsoever. He takes every opportunity to punish and threaten students. Homework assignments and projects are lengthy and boring, but relatively easy. Exams were fairly easy, but memorization heavy and unfair.

Don't know what all the hate is about. Lectures can be boring but they're super informative and well rehearsed. Grading criteria is very clear and the labs and projects are pretty easy. Weekly "pop" quizzes but they're not bad. Exams were pretty fair, minus 3 or 4 super specific questions but just attending lectures is enough to make a good grade.
reviews.ratingTags: Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment:
Talks in circles during lectures, makes everything take much longer than it needed to.
Attendance is only graded for the lab times, but there are graded assignments in each lecture (answers are given before you turn it in) so its practically mandatory attendance.
Exams are closer to trivia and fun facts than actual assessments. Avoid at all costs.

This class is a tough one, but Sal is a competent teacher. Huge stickler about phones. Knows course content very well, the workload for this class is VERY heavy. Attendance not required but random quizzes and group exercises that hurt to miss. Overall not as bad as his rating says. Better one on one than he comes off during lecture.
reviews.ratingTags: Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: DO NOT TAKE. Guy does not care about his students and thinks you're only taking his class with the amount of work he assigns.

Lamarca teaches a hard class and he is kind of dry, but he is not as bad as a lot of the reviews say he is. Definitely start projects early. The only really crazy thing was the amount of readings we were assigned, they are ungraded though. If you go to class, office hours, and are thorough with assignment submissions it's fine.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Honestly felt like these reviews are really harsh. Super easy class just boring all things considered. Socially awkward, but super passionate about what he teaches, and is clearly knowledgeable on the topic. No reason to not get an A, all labs and projects are free 100's, participation grade is also free 100 too (end of class worksheet/quiz)
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment:
yea, he gets a lot of bad reviews, but he's really just mid. He hates ppl using headphones but only has ever called out 1 or 2 so far

talks A LOT during lectures. He does clarify a lot of stuff, so that's good I guess. Hey, at least he didn't have a complete meltdown in class like a certain other CS prof who I will not name!

This guy is not nearly as bad as the reviews make him seem. He is strict about phones and headphones and DO NOT fall asleep in class. Quizzes about every other week and ALE's at least once a week. Lectures are sort of boring but helpful and use the textbook to help study for quizzes/exams. 1 midterm and 1 final exam that make up 50% of your grade.

Not as bad as people say by any means, honestly read the textbook because his teaching covers everything but is just okay especially if you don't already code a lot. Can be strict about the phone thing or anything he perceives as disrespect, but honestly a pretty easy class.

Sal is a chill guy once you get to know him. Don't let the reviews scare you. That being said, lectures are very boring and are hard to follow. To succeed, make sure to know the textbook front and back!
he's actually a good prof, like I don't understand these reviews I mean he does give lots of in class activities but its an easy class like if you study the concepts you'll be fine

Lamarca is fine. Pay attention during lectures no matter how difficult it is. Course content is easy to understand. Weighted heavily towards exams and labs so don't stress about ALEs and quizzes. Exams are straightforward and aren't designed to trick you.

Dr. LaMarca lacks in sympathy. Never met a professor that went so out of his own way to be as difficult as possible on the students. Very questionable grading criteria and tests. Asked random memorization questions such as "which page a command could be found in the C manual."
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: No one likes him. I thought he was alright, maybe underrated even. He's got wabi sabi

He is pretty strict on even pulling out your phone in class or having any type of headphones on. Also, make sure you don't sleep in his class because he WILL wake you up in front of the whole class. The projects and labs are helpful, but are tedious and his quizzes are okay. The class is very lecture heavy and he likes to do ALE which are graded.

Good lectures, but wish he did more live coding. His grading criteria is very strict, so if you forget one comment on a function, you lose about 30% on an entire lab. While he doesn't take attendance, 99% of the time there will be an in-class activity or a quiz. Read the assignment descriptions very carefully, as some have specific requirements.
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: sal's lectures are boring and could use more examples. his textbook readings are too long for you to digest any new information. he grades coding assignments very strictly. exams 50% of the course grade despite there being 4 projects and ~12 labs. he's an ok professor overall.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Test heavy

reviews.comment:
Everyone hates on him because he's strict, but given the sheer size of the class, I don't know if I can blame him *THAT* much.

Clear grading criteria (same as CS1301/2: check everything like a maniac), good at explaining, hard labs. Solid lectures, shame 70% of the class plays video games rather than listen (and yes I was part of the problem).
Horrible teacher, does not really care about his students an the difficulties that they might be facing, non negotiable.
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: this guy is like my teammates in league of legends: a bunch of inters
if i had a nickel for every time this man rambles on about how much he hates students using electronics, I would be rich cause he does this every day
Electronics are not allowed. He goes over slides and content very quickly... takes points from exam every minute you're late... will most likely have a quiz every week

If you can, take someone else. This guy has written fake rmp reviews to keep a good rating and deceive people into taking his class. He also took THIRTY points off of my lab grade for comments and indentation, even though the code worked. Don't make the same mistake I did, take someone else.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: He's a decent professor, his lectures are useful and the workload was on the heavy side of manageable but feels worse because he's so strict. Edibles help.

This guy has a bachelor's from the same school he teaches at and acts like he's hot stuff.

SOMEONE TRANSFERED MAJORS AND COLLEGES BECAUSE OF THIS GUY. DON'T TAKE HIM.

Btw higher ratings on here r just him

Always have quizzes or small exercises in class that make you have to be in class. Quizzes are frequent and annoying. reviews.ratingTags: Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes--So many papers

reviews.comment: He's super by-the-book and doesn't really teach, just reads off powerpoints. Make sure you attend class cuz even though attendance technically isn't mandatory there are assignments or quizzes every class and you'll get a 0 if you miss them. The quizzes can get very annoying, so make sure you're well-versed in the material.

Sal is fine, he's whatever. He just reads off the slides every period but the work for the class really isn't that hard, other than the final project which I thought was WAY harder than anything else we had to do, so start early on that. Make sure you follow the syllabus and the homework/project instructions to a tee otherwise you get a 0.
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: go to class bro, i didnt. i still passed so just go.

Not too sure why his rating is so low. He's pretty strict but overall he is a really solid lecturer, and the labs/projects are pretty easy. Attendance “isn't required” however there's daily graded activities meaning it's essentially required. The midterm/final are super tough as it's just a lot of material. goofy ahh professor, bro literally has a salty mood, would get crash out on us for sleeping or peeking on our phone, he mad he got no maidens, and he lashes us alot. TA's are chill doe.

reviews.comment: He knows what he is talking about but his lectures are boring. He is strict with his grading and using phones. Only 2 exams. Midterm is 50 mcq while final is 75 questions mcq. Final is hard. Exams test on memorization. Study guide he hands out for final doesn't even help for final exam.

He's pretty boring and attendance is mandatory, its whatever. What I really hate, is how labs/projects are extremely vague and he will take off 50% for an improper readme file. I had one character missing(yes it happened to me) and he did that. Exams are 75% memorization and 25% code tracing. midterm is 50 mcq and final is 75 mcq.
never have i loathed a professor like this before. wanted to do anything but go listen to him try and fill his daily word count

Really Sucks
Lectures are boring, pop quizzes or ALE's for attendance.
Really hard grader and hard tests.

This professor tries to fill a word count with every single sentence he speaks. Two exams worth 50% of the overall grade. If you are late to an exam, you get points off, so be careful. Projects and labs are not bad though. But, would never take another class with him if I have the chance, and I'd advise to get someone else if you can.

Two exams make up 50% of your grade while projects and labs only make up 30% COMBINED. A “coding” class that prioritizes memorization. Good luck getting a good grade in this class since the exams pull from the textbook and random in class statements that are never mentioned twice.
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Teaches off of slides (sometimes code), and knows what he's talking about. Attendance is not taken, but graded handouts and pop quizzes are given almost every class. The exams are okay, just expect for there to be material that is only found in the textbooks (yes, two). There is a lab assignment every week, and 4 projects throughout the semester.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Coming in to the class I expected the antichrist with how people talk about him. Genuinely if you just do the projects and follow each guideline (they're pretty clear) then you'll be plenty fine. Best professor I've had in the computer science department at UGA so far.
I never wore my seatbelt while driving to school because I wanted to die before making it to his class.
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: He reads from powerpoints or codes in front of the class. There is little actual teaching. Its not really an issue at first because the topics are simple and easy to grasp but as they get harder his lack of teaching becomes more apparent. I learned far more in lab than in lecture. He has active learning assignments randomly to ensure attendance.
save yourself and do not take this professor.

Not understanding when it comes to grading. Will give you a 0 if you make any error in your code despite the instructions being unclear. Will rather tell you to "use it as a learning experience" instead of hearing what you have to say. Lab every week & you will have weeks where you have a lab, project, and test all in same week. Not too hard tho

Reviews are kinda dramatic. Of course if there's a project you have to do it his way... That goes for any class. They're not particularly difficult. Expectations and assignments are straightforward. If you really think this class is so unreasonable, CS may not be a good choice for you. He's a chill guy. Just study and do your work

Definitely over hated, but wouldn't say he's amazing, just okay. Didn't really pay attention, just read the textbook and notes that he posted. Attendance is mandatory, and he has pop quizzes and group activities based on what he just taught in class. Projects and labs are easier than those in 1302. Tests are difficult. Take him if only option.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Look at the review distribution and tell me nothing is wrong here. He's not fooling a bunch of CS and math students with this. Everybody knows how he is as a prof and still takes him because he's the only option. He needs to listen to the bad reviews rather than dismiss them with his colossal ego

I've heard a lot of negative things about him, but imo he was decent. He just is very picky over project criteria and honestly does not give a lot of grace if you miss a quiz/lecture. Had a friend that made a super small error on his project and ended up getting a 0. Just be sure to follow project instructions clearly and you should be good.
Take course over the Summer with a different professor to keep your sanity. The United Healthcare of UGA CS trying to deny you from getting into the major.
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: i could fill a book with my complaints. pettiest prof ever. made a 41 question study guide for the final and only gave it to the 3 people who stayed for his lecture the day before fall break and refused to post it online. if ur stuck with him i sincerely apologize, read the sections he assigns thoroughly bc you will not learn anything from lecture.
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Participation matters--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Great lecturer, not the nicest person. Definitely knows his stuff. I had no experience with C before this but have done just fine. Pop quiz and ALE practically every week. Projects are easy, just start on them early. Most labs are straightforward, but 2 or 3 are trickier. Exams aren't awful, but he throws in some random things you don't expect.
Course itself is already hard but its not too bad since I had prior knowledge on C. But he always wants everything done HIS way which made me spend an unnecessary amount of time on certain projects.
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: I didn't even take his class I just had to constantly listen to my boyfriend complain about him and so now I have beef with him
In NBA terms, Lamarca is the Dillon Brooks of the CS department at UGA.
Lectures are soo boring. You're basically have to teach yourself everything because he's no help at all
I mean does he expect a bunch of computer science students to look at this distribution of reviews and believe it???

Professor Lamarca cares more about his image than his students. He boasts about being tough, but it's just a front for bad teaching. Half the class dropped out by midterm. Save yourself some time and don't enroll.

This man thrives on chaos. The syllabus was a mess, assignments were poorly explained, and he made it clear that our struggles were not his problem. If you enjoy confusion, stress, and absolute indifference, this is the class for you. Otherwise, RUN.

With a name like Salvatore Lamarca, you'd expect to be at least somewhat of a decent human being, but somehow this guy couldn't live up to the bare minimum expectations. TERRIBLE teacher. A WASTE of an awesome name. And doesn't care about students welfare, mental health, or performance in his class. DO NOT TAKE.

Decent professor but horrible person. Can NEVER skip class cuz one day you'll have a quiz and other day of the week an ALE. Lectures are boring but if you pay attention, he explains well. Labs every week (13 total), 4 projects, and 10 quizzes + ALEs. He doesn't encourage questions at all so dont even bother.

After reading all the reviews on here, I was pretty nervous about taking his class. However, he's not as bad as everyone says he is. He's a pretty good but boring teacher. However, he is easy to talk to in terms of anything class related or cs related. You can tell he is passionate about CS. Yes, it is a hard class so be prepared and start early.
reviews.ratingTags: Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: As other reviews mention, he is very strict and not a very fuzzy person. However, he knows what he's talking about and explains concepts well. Weekly hw, 4 projs, midterm, final. Good teacher and the course is well organized, but he may not be your favorite person ever.
The lectures are dry, but if you stay engaged and use helpful study tools like unstuckstudy ai, you can do well. Lots of work, but manageable if you keep up.

Occasionally tests for irrelevant knowledge - always tests for intimate knowledge. One MCQ read, "What command was page X of the manual?". Wants his students to come to class, but instead of taking attendance, just doesn't upload material. One of his favorite pastimes is drinking students' tears on RMP and writing/coaxing fake reviews.

took in Spring 2024 and his class is straightforward. To succeed, simply attend classes regularly, be prepared for pop quizzes, complete ALEs, show up to labs, and stay on top of your work. If you do, you'll find that his examples are super helpful for homework and projects. Lamarca may come across as strict, but trust me, you'll learn a ton!

Beware of Mr. Lamarca's fragile ego! He doesn't allow phones out during lectures, and if you look at him the wrong way while he's chastising another student, he might go after you too! He doesn't take attendance for lectures, but there are frequent in-class pop quizzes. The "lab" is just a homework session with graded attendance.

reviews.comment: I would never take him again!
Don't take him!

reviews.comment: He wrote all the fake 5-star reviews. His lectures put you to sleep. His grading rubric is nitpicky. He is mean. On day one, he said not to come to him if we have mental health issues from his class because he isn't trained to handle that. Pop quizzes. Avoid this man at all costs unless you know the material already. Bad look on the CS department

He writes his own reviews. Avoid him, unless you already know the class material. He is a mean person. On the first day of class, he told people who have mental health problems from his class that they should not come to him, because he is not trained to handle that. His grading rubrick is tough and nitpicky. His tests were mostly fair.

Seems like a chill person. Unfortunately, that doesn't carry over to his teaching. Vague covering of the readings, very slow paced, and leaves you with little to no information about assignments. ~12-13 labs, 6 quizzes, 4 projects, a midterm and a final. Very rigorous class, so stay on top of your work and finish labs & projects as soon as you can.

Systems was smooth sailing as I had experience with almost all concepts. Even so, most of the LaMarca criticism is overblown and wasn't apparent to me. I really recommend attending the lectures and dedicating an appropriate amount of time to the Labs and Projects, they are fun. Course is easier if you have genuine interest in some of the material.

Knowledgeable professor, but very monotonous lectures. Lots of assignments that aren't always the easiest (weekly labs and also projects that overlap with the labs). At least one pop quiz and active learning every week. Exams are okay but study every single little detail in the textbook/powerpoints. Class can be very hard if you don't keep up.
reviews.ratingTags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Lamarca isn't as bad as people make him out to be. More difficult than 1301/1302 but very doable to get an A if you pay attention and keep up with the assignments
reviews.ratingTags: Clear grading criteria--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

Extra credit at end.

reviews.comment: This is not a fun class. It's difficult with a heavy workload and an uninspiring professor who gives pop quizzes on eLC at the end of class. reviews.comment: Dawg reported and got my first review removed. Says a lot about the dude.

I would say I'm pretty indifferent if not slightly amiable with Sal. He's pretty chill in this upper level course as well as being amicable, chatty, and overall much more easygoing. reviews.comment: Everyone talks trash about Sal but he is actually a good teacher. If you have him for 1301 he will be tough but in higher level classes he is very helpful and gives you all the tools you need to pass the class. Show up to his class, start on the homework early and crack a joke in his class. He is not as bad as people make him out to be. I promise.

Attendance is not mandatory, though ALEs and quizzes are given at random. Lots of content is covered in this course, though equations and notes are not posted and must be written down during class time. Do not get me wrong, taking notes will definitely help as exams and quizzes are open-note and open-MATLAB. But personally, would not recommend.

Probably the most insufferable and boring person I have ever had to be in the presence of in my life. Avoid him at all costs you will want to drop out of school and give up. Really really really annoying

I thoroughly enjoyed my time in this course with Dr. Sal, The content was interesting and he was available to discuss questions after class. Due to the smaller class size I felt that there was more time available for the students to receive help from Dr. Sal personally. I even showed up after my final ended and Dr. Sal let me take it anyways.

1 student of ~80 in the class was on his phone, Sal scolded him in front of everyone. Another guy far in the back just shook his head disapprovingly, scolded him too. Treats students like middle-schoolers. Not v helpful when answering questions. If you're smart you'll probably get a good grade regardless, but he should not be your first pick.
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Beware of pop quizzes--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: My only gripe with him is that he requires attendance and only gives out 2 skips, besides that he's a PHENOMENAL professor

I really liked the memory diagrams that Dr. Cotterell drew in class for variables and objects and stuff and the way he explained things. I also liked how he emphasized figuring out how to do something ourselves before trying to get the computer to do it, because approaching the labs and projects that way was a game changer for me. Great professor.

need to be passionate about cs or you will fail. this class is 100% a weed out class and made me realize i didn't want to pursue cs. but cotterell is a really good lecturer and explains concepts very well and overall a good professor to have for fundamentals but regardless still a hard class!
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Cotterall was a great teacher as for the course its self make sure to keep up with the projects based off of the timeline they give you and for exams go through the entire textbook and do practice problems from there.

Professor Cotterell is a wonderful professor who brings good energy and passion to teaching everyday! He explains concepts clearly and answers questions well. Overall, 1302 is a difficult and time-consuming course but you cannot go wrong with either Professor Cotterell or Barnes.
reviews.ratingTags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Dr. Barnes was SO knowledgeable about every topic discussed & was an effective lecturer/teacher. Super well structured class. Lectures easy to follow & he will work with you. Tests are the hardest I've ever taken, I failed most despite studying hard (I am anxious), so be beware if that's you. Projects were clear but violations are harsh. Take him.

Cotterell and Barnes are the GOATS of CS. Cotterell wants you to master the basics, and is very good at drawing out advanced concepts into simple diagrams. Tests are incredibly hard if you don't go to class, as there aren't any online slides or study guides. Lots of readings and projects, but they're well designed and you will learn a lot.

TAKE HIM. Absolutely could not have taken this class and done well without him. Lectures are super boring but he does an amazing job at explaining concepts and the textbook (free) is extremely useful. Tests are a somewhat difficult but he allows a full page of notes as a cheat sheet. Don't procrastinate projects.

As long as you do your work ahead of the due date and don't wait till the last minute, you're fine. Projects are time-consuming but easy. Tests are definitely harder, but if you actually pay attention in class, you'll do fine. Overall very easy-going professor and really helpful when you have trouble with concepts and projects.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: he's just a chill dude
Class is very organized, great lecturer, but they are a little boring. The class can be hard if you decide to procrastinate
Great prof. The course has a bit of an insane workload, but he eases that a bit with his good examples and explanations. Understanding and kind person, willing to work with you. Take him if you can!!

Great teacher, could teach quantum physics to an elementary schooler. Class just has a very high workload, with constant readings, homeworks, and projects to be doing/working on. Tests are also extremely application-based, so in order to do well you need to go out of your way to find and actually PRACTICE examples that are harder than the given.

Goated professor who is so helpful, but man this class was brutal. Drop the ego from 1301, this class will push you to your limits. 3 tests make up 55% of your grade (quite difficult); don't skip TB reading—it's the only thing that will save you besides office hours (very helpful👍). Procrastination will haunt you if you don't start projects early.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I wish I'd had Michael Cotterell earlier—he's the GOAT. Great lectures, free textbook, and quick homework done in ten minutes (minimum). I never studied for tests and still aced them by attending class and doing HW. The Java/Unix content is easy and fun. Quizzes and homework are online. Tests are also online with secure browser.
just not dr.barnes,.

Dr. Cotterell is one of the nicest and most caring professors i've had so far at UGA. He tries to make his lectures as entertaining as possible and is willing to work with you and help you out as best he can. He is definitely the best CS teacher, however, your TA is the one who mainly grades your labs and projects. Work on assignments ahead of time

Great lecturer and best of all the CS teachers, just a hard class (depending on your background). There's a lot of extra credit to make up for the difficulty. One thing I'd recommend (which he recommends too) is working on the projects way early. They take longer than you'd expect, and one misunderstanding can cost you 30+% so really double check.
reviews.ratingTags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I always felt like I could ask Dr. Coterell a question and that he really cared whether I understood the response. He is really good at explaining stuff and super helpful in office hours. I don't think I have learned as much in any other class. I hope he teaches some other classes in the future so that I can take a class from him again!

Dr. Cotterell was great! He wrote code live during lecture, explained things well, and helped me during office hours the two or three times that I went. He didn't use power points but did post really good notes about what he covered in class and example code on elc most weeks. 1 midterm, 12 labs, 4 projects, some activities and quizzes and a final.

Great explainer and caring, but not as active with learning during lecture. Gets quite boring without a background in CS. Expects learning to be done from textbook and only shows 1 long example in class. Tests and quizzes are understandable if you understand the concepts. Don't feel confident creating code, but understand coding concepts well!

He's a pretty good professor & lecturer; he wants you to understand what's going on & is happy to answer questions. 3 tests & 3 projects + a final project. Not too hard if you know CS. If you don't, just make sure to start assignments early & then ask questions/go to office hours.

Dr cotterell is an amazing prof but you really need to try hard in this class or you will fail. Get the work done as early as possible after he assigns it and you'll honestly be fine. Get the projects done asap. The tests aren't bad and the homework isn't bad. Read the project guidelines very carefully tho

Dr. Cotterell is a fantastic CS lecturer. I went into his class with no prior knowledge and his lectures made learning the material easy. The labs and projects are tedious and can be frustrating, especially later in the semester, but the department creates those, not him. Amazing professor, would definitely recommend.

GOATed experience. He emphasized planning before coding and coding "up" the entire semester. Not gonna lie, I didn't really agree with or understand what he meant until after a few assignments, but after trying to approach stuff the way he recommended, it's based.
Cotterell's lectures are thorough and detailed. While they can be a bit tough, the homeworks and projects are manageable. unstuck ai was a huge help with study prep.

He is the best. I've never learned so much in one class. Tests are fair, and homeworks/projects will push you. This class made me sure that I wanted to be a CS major. He lectures very well for a CS class.
This class really killed me, but cotterell, he is the goat. If you ever have questions just go to office hours its literally amazing.
reviews.ratingTags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Great at explaining and very caring. If you already know programming (especially Java) this will be a piece of cake - I got 100% on everything (including all labs and exams) besides one quiz. Midterm, final, and labs were easy. You can learn from the textbook, but lectures have activities and quizzes each week (although they're very easy).

Cotterell is super smart and that plays into his lectures. His lectures can be ever so slightly hard to sit through, especially when compared to Barnes. But nevertheless, his lectures are super useful. Homeworks are easy and are somewhat guided by tutorials and the projects are long.

Your experience will probably be the same no matter which 1302 prof you take, their assignments and deadlines are the same. lots of extra credit including up to 10 extra points on each project. final project instead of exam.  lectures are thorough and detailed. not hard to get an A if you submit all the assignments. all three exams are online.
reviews.ratingTags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: I am not a computer science major, but I did need this class for a prerequisite. With that being said, I struggled in the class constantly. The labs are tough and you have to put a lot of time into them to get a good grade. The TA's are very picky, but it's their job to be. Pay attention in class and read the textbook. It will be your best friend.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Great professor! The best way to be prepared for his exams are to read the textbook and review the notes he posts. The lectures were a lot easier than than what was covered in the textbook but both are very important. He's very kind and patient, so don't be afraid to ask questions! Good luck!

Professor Cotterell brings a lot of energy into his lectures, even when the class vibe is dead 24/7. Great professor that will take his time to make sure you understand. What I really appreciated was that he taught as if we had never taken a CS course before and makes sure to explain any CS jargon that he uses. A beginner friendly course, take him!

Review got deleted- 25% midterm, 25% final, labs, projects, and quizzes takes over the rest. Cotterell is the best intro to comp sci prof to take- the notes that he posts on eLC are super helpful (STUDY THEM!!). Textbook required but can be found in libgen. Helpful for vocab and mc questions on the tests. Doable class- lab attendance required.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: One of the GOATs of 1302 along with Dr. Barnes. He explains things really well and is great at answering questions. Take him if you have a chance.

. He explains everything so clearly and pretty much everything you need is in the readings he assigns. HWs are not too bad. Projects get easier as you progress through the class because you'll be able to understand documentation quicker. Exams are a little hard but doable. Lots of extra credit.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Dr. Cotterell really takes the time to help you  is an excellent teacher

He isn't a bad teacher however, I wasn't a big fan of his teaching style. I took a course which let me have one cs teacher for half the term and cotteral for the second half. The change in teachers and teaching style may have been a factor in not liking the teaching style as much. The lectures are complicated but useful.

This class way easy, if you read assign chapters from the textbook you will be fine. My biggest issue though was Labs, Labs follow a universal syllabus, you get one submission for most of the labs. It kinda unfair because other professors gave unlimited submissions for labs. That was my biggest complaint. Overall a good class, i guess.
Lectures are great! He teaches by writing code during class AND is really good at explaining what is going on.
Dr. Cotterell does a great job explaining programming concepts. I really like how he writes code with us during class.
Awesome!

Good lectures that are clear if you pay attention. Class is a lot of work and a lot of reading but everything is laid out step by step and very clear so you can answer most of your own questions just by reading detailed instructions everywhere!
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: The class was very straightforward and provided great learning oppurtunities. Cotterell explains the topics well. His office hours are early in the morning so I recommend going to the afternoon office hours since more TAs are available to help. Projects should be started ASAP especially since bonus pts are given for early submissions.

laughable at how simple this course was, but wtv. basically just read ~10mins before lectures and try the concepts on your own and youll be good. everythings on github so clear grading criteria and readings are p good. he's a nice guy & teaches p well. online Q&A (piazza) was good also. cotterell & barnes got this course down.

He's awesome, you can't go wrong by taking his class. He is very good at explaining hard concepts durring the lectures. Project/homework instructions are also very detailed and usually walk you through the hardest parts.
Really easy class with some fun reading assignments, recitations & defense summaries took a bit of time each week but wasn't anything too hard.

1302 is pretty much going to be the same whether it's Barnes or Cotterell, clear expectations of what is needed to pass or what is going to be in the exams. Great class that teaches you the fundamentals of OOP.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria--Respected

reviews.comment: A difficult class, but if you ask questions, he's very keen to understand and help you understand them mid-lecture. Also very helpful during Office Hours.

He is a really good professor, but you have to do the readings & pay attention in class. lectures are kind of like the readings. It's honestly a pretty easy class if you start working on projects/hw early. 5 projects, hw pretty much every week, lots of extra credit tho

Cotterell is a great professor. He explains the topics very well and makes sure that everyone understands them. However, the workload can be a bit extreme at times. 5 projects throughout the semester was absurd, and seemed like overkill. On top of this, there are homework assignments almost every week to complete, as well as 3 exams.

Cotterell devoted in-class time throughout the semester to work on our team term project, worked with each team one-on-one to answer questions, and had an interesting and engaging class structure that basically allowed us to discuss what we were learning about in class each and every class period. No programming, but very interesting and engaging.

1302 was a very tough class. Cotterell was a great professor and cared about his students so despite it being difficult I was able to do well and pass with an A. Lots of extra credit. Highly recommend this professor for any CS class.

Always easy to reach online. Projects were easy and you get 10 bonus points for submitting 2 days early. Tests were fine, and homework was easy too.
Didn't attend many lectures, but the ones I went to were well taught and did a good job covering the material.

If you have the opportunity to take this class with Dr. Cotterell, please do. I cannot recommend him enough; he is both incredibly knowledgeable about the class, but also really cares about helping students reach where they want to be. Please please take him if you can-you will thank yourself for it.

The content in this class is difficult, but Dr. Cotterell can explain them well and helps outside of class. There are also many office hours and extra credit opportunities. However, there are so many readings and assignments in this class, and I felt like I could never catch up. Overall a great professor- just a tough class with a lot of work.
reviews.ratingTags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I feel like the class could be taught a little bit better.  Theres readings before every class period and in class he more or less goes over the readings, which feels a little redundant.  Overall good teacher, gives extra credit if you turn the projects in early and if you go to class

Dr. Cotterell is a great professor especially when it comes to debugging when you run into issues with your code. No problem that took him more than a few seconds to solve. 1302 is a hard class. You will be learning a lot of the nitty-gritty of coding in Java. Along with this be prepared to use a Unix environment, not Eclipse.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Dr. Supa Mike is easily the best CS professor I've had at UGA. His lectures are very helpful and informative, and he is very good at breaking down complex topics into examples. The projects for this class can be very demanding and frustrating at times, but don't give up and maybe go to office hours and you will succeed.

Had to study a lot outside of class, lectures are just there to supplement. Quizzes/tests are hard, but grading of projects is SO MUCH more generous than 1301. He's a great lecturer, funny guy, but his voice is a bit annoying and gets monotone. Barnes was a lot more helpful during office hours. Make sure to start your code early for projects/hw!
reviews.ratingTags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Cotterell is a really good prof! The projects were difficult but going to lecture, staying on top of the material, and def going to office hours is the best way to do well on them. The exams were always very fair. Beware of the first minesweeper project. It is hard but def doable START EARLY. They really have set up this class to help you succeed
Very good teacher but the content is a little challenging. If you show up to class and do the extra credit you will do good. Also very accessible outside of class and a lot of office hours.
reviews.ratingTags: EXTRA CREDIT--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Amazing professor but the class was very difficult. Projects are extremely hard, time-consuming, and stressful but if you work hard and pay attention I think you'll be fine. But seriously, the projects made me want to rip my hair out.

Dr. Cotterell is great. He's passionate and cares about learning. Every day, he not only writes, compiles, and runs working and good looking code from scratch to demo what we're learning about, but he breaks it down, explains it well, and answers questions about it all at the same time. This way that he teaches is enjoyable and refreshing.

Cotterell was amazing during my time with him. All the concepts were laid out in a format that was easily accessible and understandable with walkthrough exercises to help you get a solid foundation under your feet for the projects. Do the work, and you will not only pass the class but come out feeling like you learned something from Cotterell.

Dr. Cotterell is hands-down the best professor I've ever had. Although his class consists of some hard to understand topics, he make sure everyone understands the concepts and provides all the resources you need to build your foundation. There's lots of extra-credit and he actually makes class enjoyable and interesting (unlike other CS professors).

This class was probably the hardest class I've taken here so far. It actually made me decide to switch majors. There's a ton of work. With that said, Professor Cotterell was great. He was friendly, helpful, and when he could tell students were struggling with projects, he would even extend deadlines. Great professor, just not a fan of the class.

Dr. Cotterell is a great professor. His course is extremely well-organized and he treats you like a human being. While his class is rigorous and major projects can be difficult, he gives extra credit for turning them in early and is often available to help via Piazza or office hours. Make sure to start on weekly assignments and projects early.

While this class is a weed-out, an A is very possible. Dr. Cotterell explains the material well, but do mind that this is a sink or swim type of class. Start projects early and read the material before each class. There are tons of other students, so do not expect much 1 on 1 help. It will help to be familiar with Emacs before you start.

Dr. Cotterell is an amazing professor. He makes all of the material easy to understand and the course is very organized. That being said, it is a lot of work at times but if you stay on top of it, 1302 doesn't have to be hard at all. Just follow the course layout, study the material before every exam, and start projects/homework early!

While this was the most difficult class I took this semester and I struggled a lot, I thought Dr. Cotterell was a very good professor. He made sure to take the time to answer student questions and to explain things in different ways if we didn't understand it the first time. He provided a lot of support and I would definitely recommend him.
reviews.ratingTags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Go to class, do the readings, take the quizzes and do the homework. This is a small part of your grade, but you will learn a lot. The projects are really enjoyable if you like coding, but can be time-consuming. There's also a Piazza board the professors respond to often. The tests aren't bad if you understand the quizzes. A cheatsheet is allowed.
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Dr. Cotterell was by far the best CS professor that I have ever had. He gives great lectures that go over the material that is on the test. His projects are time consuming, but not impossible. The weekly quizzes and homework was annoying at times but nothing that was not manageable. I would highly recommend you take Dr. Cotterell!

Very difficult and time consuming class, but taught by one of the best professors at this school. Due to the nature of java, much of it is impossible for you to understand just by watching the lectures so you'll more than likely have to do all the readings and tutorials. Start early on projects because they can easily take upwards of 15 hours.
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Good professor overall - Cotterell definitely made the concepts that he taught understandable to everyone. He (and Barnes) also gave project/deadline extensions sometimes. The course materials were hosted on GitHub which helped for accessibility. However, there is a LOT of work in this class, and you need to be prepared to work your butt off.

Cotterell is very knowledgeable and available. However, he is also very unaccommodating, lacking the proper discretion for extenuating circumstances like the pandemic. I suppose that is to be expected of a weed-out class like this, though. He and his TAs grade incredibly harsh, beware of losing entire letter grades over very minor technicalities.

Cotterell is a fantastic teacher and communicator. I can say I've never learned so much in a semester before. BEWARE: Cotterell & Barnes team up and combine the 1302 classes into 1 huge class. CSCI1302 IS A WEED-OUT CLASS. These two men take this class super seriously and grade pretty hardcore. But again, their class makes 1301 look like a joke.

Hard class, especially during a pandemic. It keeps you busy with an insane amount of work. Both teachers did the best they couldve, but due to the nature of this semester, there were plenty of times where I felt overwhelmed or not supported. He is helpful, but I was not a fan of the reverse classroom. TAs were great & projects are SO important!
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Dr. Cotterell has a great way of walking through and explaining examples during class. Before this class, I had never written a single line of code. Now I find myself writing code for fun! Definitely take this class with Dr. Cotterell!

Dr. Cotterel wrote most of the readings, which was great because it meant it directly related to other assignments. This semester, we were asked to read and do an exercise before each class, then ask questions as Dr. Cotterall demos / codes the important parts of the exercise live in class. He's enthusiastic and great at explaining things.

Fine as a lecturer, but condescending at times. Doesn't really teach that much, but that is marketed as the gimmick of the class (that you learn everything yourself). So if you're debating whether to take Cotterell or Barnes (two current lecturers), here's my answer: it doesn't matter. You'll be learning everything yourself anyway!

This class wakes you up. You cant slack off, must attend to work for a participation grade, and must start projects early. Dr. Cotterell is very caring and intelligent, and it's obvious that he wants his students to learn and truly grasp the material. This class grows you as programmer more than any other, so try to enjoy the hustle.
reviews.ratingTags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.--Inspirational

reviews.comment: Dr. Cotterell was very accessible and constantly answered questions. The course was graded mostly based on your ability to perform on exams with the midterm and final totaling 60% of your grade. If you perform poorly on exams, like me; then don't take this professor, because even 100+ on homeworks and quizzes won't save your grade.
reviews.ratingTags: Gives good feedback--Graded by few things--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Can't say enough good things about Dr. Cotterell. He made easy to follow and high quality videos for us, which made the material much easier to grasp. He was very accessible through Piazza and always provided helpful responses. He also gave us tons of extra credit opportunities. Definitely take this class with him if you can.

This is my second time taking a class with Dr. Cotterell. In this case, the class schedule and grading criteria was so clear and that was really appreciated. It's definitely an interesting class, and reading the textbook is a must, but it's a good read!
reviews.ratingTags: Accessible outside class--Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Dr. Cotterell graded the midterm instead of the TAs, and it's honestly some of the best feedback I've ever gotten on an assignment (I got an A-). Way better than the TAs. He also records videos for us to watch after doing the readings so that we can work on the material at our own pace. I like the way he presents stuff compared to the book.

As 1302 goes, it's probably the hardest cs class at UGA period. So I'd advise buckling down. Cotterell is great, and he explains the tough concepts well. My class was flipped, so there were never any long lectures, 10-15 minutes at most. The rest of the classes are doing coding exercises with partners. But this will be the hardest class you take.
reviews.ratingTags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Participation matters

reviews.comment: This class wasn't easy, and the workload was often heavy, but it certainly improved my programming ability greatly. Also, Mike clearly shows great care and understanding towards his students, as evidenced by the multitude of available office hours all semester and course adjustments made after the switch to online. Put in the work and you've got it

Skipped some assignments and still going to make an A-. Topics were interesting, and the work isn't hard--just have to do it. I really like the different ethical theories we used in the class. Dr. Cotterell was great!

Cotterell offers great perspectives into the material and is an all around likable professor. If you do the work, then you'll pass the class. While the work can seem tedious at times, many of the topics are really fun and interesting.

tons of online busywork. Only got worse with the switch to online classes. Didn't even record actual lectures, just gave us youtube videos to watch. The final included 2 essays (with a max word count of 1500) that we were expected to write in the middle of finals week.
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Dr. Cotterell truly cares about his students. I am currently taking CSCI1302 and it changed to online. This class rocks. Class exercises + weekly quizzes are expected to be completed. Projects, they're tough and you need to learn how to problem solve given a skeleton outline (constantly on google). The workload is a step up from 1301 but its doable
reviews.ratingTags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Honestly loving Dr. Cotterells flipped class. Its way better than my 1301 experience. Work isnt bad if you stay on schedule and there are lots of office hours when you need help.

Dr. Cotterell is friendly and knowledgeable. At first I didn't know how to manage my time with his class, but I took his suggestions seriously about how to work through the material, and it paid off. He was also great at answering questions in class and in office hours. He even incorporated our feedback into the class, which was cool.

The CS department is really working to improve their teaching, and Cotterell showed us this by taking our feedback halfway through the semester then instantly implementing it. Hard projects but coding doesn't come natural to me so may be easier for others. Amazing lecturer the few times he did lecture but mostly group work everyday.
This class was a lot of work, but I also learned a lot. Dr. Cotterell was readily accessible in class and during office hours. Class was flipped.

The class was flipped, but Dr. Cotterell was able to answer all of my questions when I had some. Really good at explaining things. Final project was a group project. My partner and I made our own version of asteroids and mancala!
reviews.ratingTags: Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Great guy. Just took the last test and feel prepared to work through the final project. Cotterell is really good at explaiing things, which he had to do for me alot. Extra credit is nice too.

Dr. Cotterell's class is rough if you don't keep up, but if you do the class exercises and ask for help when you're stuck, then it's not bad at all. He's also really nice and helpful during office hours.
Great professor! Chill, knowledgeable, and respectful. Make sure you do the readings from the (free, online) textbook!

Late posting, but had Dr. Barnes fall of 2025. He's great! Very chill, knowledgeable, and accessible. Make sure you do the readings from the textbook (the textbook is free, online, and by the professors) before each class to be ready. Content can be tough if you don't have prior experience besides 1301, but you can do it!
Best professor I've had so far. Expect to work super hard in this class, but the resources you need to succeed are available to you and you will come out knowing you've learned a lot.

to the guy who gave a 1.0 because he submitted a project late, have you considered that maybe... just maybe, that it's a skill issue? This guy was the goat and the way he explained stuff made it sound so easy, this class was geniunely the easiest course of my life. He is also very organized so that helped alot too.

reviews.comment: Everyday walking from my dorm to class I was praying that the central east bus would hit me and i would have a fatal accident to avoid going to this so called professor's class. reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Barnes is great, you'll be incredibly happy with him! Textbook is free, lecture materials posted online, and he's able to teach some more complex topics in a simple and entertaining way. That is not to say the class doesn't require effort, however. Barnes is a great professor for what can be a very difficult class if you don't stay on top of it

Barnes is a great professor, he's super chill and understanding. This class requires you to attend every class and do the readings to get a good grade. The projects can be difficult but there's a lot of extra credit. 3 exams, each pretty hard, so do the readings. No final exam, just a final project. He takes attendance through daily activities.
Difficult class but good professor so that helps.
reviews.ratingTags: EXTRA CREDIT--Amazing lectures --Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: Coming up to the end of this class, Prof. Barnes was probably the best possible teacher you could have had for this course. If you're a prospective CS or CSE major and you want to really learn, you have all the resources to do so. Lots of help on projects and relatively easy tests if you read. Grades are merciful and good TA's in office hours.

He is rude and inconsiderate but popular with certain students bc he is something they can relate to. He's not more entertaining than other cs profs but "clearer" to understand so that must be why he's deemed so funny. Shamed an international student in front of the class for asking when our final would be so she could buy her ticket home.

Dr. Barnes was truly a great professor; I had Mr. Bass for 1301 and had a lot of difficulty because he wasn't a great lecturer at all. It was so much easier to understand Dr. Barnes and him and Dr. Cotterell wrote a whole book for this course which is GREAT for understanding. The class is structured so well and projects and Homeworks are helpful

Barnes is a great profesor. He gives engaging lectures and he sets clear expectations and guidelines in regards to grading. 1302 has a lot of work and requires a lot of time. If you work diligently on your projects and review for the tests you should find success. Good luck!
reviews.ratingTags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Great lecturer that made class fun & engaging. Textbook readings required before each class & occasional pop quizzes/polls. Textbook is well written and easy to understand. Tests are quite difficult and are randomly announced about a week before. Projects & homework can be difficult, but gives good help in class and office hours.
reviews.ratingTags: Amazing lectures --Beware of pop quizzes--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: I'd definitely recommend taking this professor for 1302. The projects can be difficult, but he gave us hints for how to do them + extra pts if u submit them early. When I went to him for advice 1-1, I didn't get the impression that he belittles students. He talks in a straightforward chill way, so i guess it might come across that way to some ppl?
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Barnes is an okay teacher and the class is structured fairly, but tough. If you take a lightish class load, you can pass with all the extra credit he gives. However, Dr. Barnes is one of the rudest professors I have come across on campus. He belittles his students in office hours regularly. If you must take him, avoid 1-1 interactions.

Dr. Barnes is one of the greatest teachers I have had at UGA. He knows the material and is passionate about it, yet explains everything in an easy to understand way. This class is difficult but he is very helpful during office hours and gives lots extra credit so the difficulty was easily overlooked by how great this class was.
reviews.ratingTags: EXTRA CREDIT--Amazing lectures --Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: he is the sexiest professor i have ever laid eyes on in my 21 years of living.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Dr. Barnes once said during lecture that he was more interested in instruction rather than research, and it shows! There is never a dull moment during his lectures, and he is just as passionate during his office hours (which I highly recommend)! Although lots of assignments are assigned, he also has lots of extra credit opportunities.
One of the best professors i've ever had. knows how to teach and explains it in a way everyone will be able to understand. highly recommend.

The best professor I have ever had at UGA. He really will take your programming knowledge up a notch and he makes it all very manageable. Just be prepared to do a fair amount of work outside of class in order to have a full grasp on what he is talking about during class.

Very good professor who explains things well and always answers questions. 10 extra credit points if you turn in projects early. Cheat sheet allowed on tests. A bit work-heavy, but it is necessary for the content. No laptops allowed in class, but he won't say anything if you write on a tablet (the rule is actually helpful in my opinion).

100% take Dr. Barnes if you can. He is a great lecturer; he knows the material super well, answers questions efficiently, grades fairly, and owns up to his mistakes. He co-wrote the textbook so his lectures fit with the assigned reading. He has made my list of my top 3 favorite teachers of my life, and I'd give anything to take his class again.
the goat of computer science.
Very helpful. Willing to work with you through all the issues in your code. Not a very difficult class if you just do the work.

This course challenged me so much that it made me reconsider my major. However, Barnes does his absolute best to make sure you understand tough concepts. He's funny which made the lectures engaging. Extra credit polls for going to class and doing the readings. Best advice is to stay on top of homework and start projects early for extra credit.

Class is extremely time-consuming. I would recommend not taking other heavy courses with it. Additionally, their new textbook is helpful & free. 

Dr. Barnes doesn't allow phones/computers in class, but he is a very charismatic person. I never felt any need to look at my phone. He explains topics extremely well and is helpful.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Dr. Barnes is by far the best professor I've had so far. Computer Science is just not an easy subject whatsoever, but Barnes really put in his best effort to make sure we understood and was also always really good at keeping us engaged in the lectures. Make sure you actually read the textbook, which is handwritten by him and is free!

this was definitely a hard class but barnes did a great job with it, and made a daunting subject feel way more approachable. he's super chill and i really enjoyed his lectures. do your best to turn in the projects early for extra credit as those 5-10 extra points can help a lot.

Professor Barnes is a fantastic lecturer who makes complex CS concepts easy to grasp. Though the workload is heavy, his engaging teaching style, available office hours, and optional study tools like Unstuck ai make this course very rewarding. Start projects early and leverage all resources for an A.

Homework every week, alongside 4 projects, 3 exams, and a final project. Gives lots of extra credit for showing up, as well as submitting projects early. Start projects the day they're assigned, and make sure you use office hours whenever you need the slightest of help. Final project should really be done as early as possible. Amazing professor.
reviews.ratingTags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Dr. Barnes is a great professor and his lectures are very helpful and engaging! Offers lots of extra credit - submitting projects early and points from in-class polls. Lots of work assigned - stay on top of everything and if you submit projects early you will do well! Tests are fair, but you must pay attention to lecture material and big concepts.
reviews.ratingTags: Amazing lectures --Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Barnes is a great lecturer. All lectures are super engaging and useful. He doesn't allow laptops out during lectures, but something like an iPad for notes is okay. Homeworks are easy and are somewhat guided via tutorials. Projects are somewhat time-consuming but are useful for learning.

Had him a year ago and is still the best professor I've had at UGA. His lecture style with readings outside of class is a great format for 1302 and helped me learn a lot. Projects can be difficult but as long as you start them early and ask for help when needed you'll get an A. Gives lots of bonus points as well

Dr. Barnes is far and away the most fantastic professor that I have had at the University of Georgia. He explains every concept very well and is always available after class for help in coding and other assignments. My biggest advice for those who take 1302 is to take advantage of office hours. Barnes is the best. Take him.

My class was made last second. It was taught by barnes before spring break and by cottrell after spring break. Barnes is a lot better of a teacher since he teaches his lessons on the board as opposed to cottrell who jumps straight into code. Barnes is also pretty funny and has engaging lectures. I definitely recommend him over cottrell.

I loved having class with him. The class is difficult, however he makes it easier to understand and breaks everything down so that even a toddler can understand it. Extremely helpful and the lectures are very lively. I highly recommend taking him for 1302.
I had no coding experience except for 1301. He is the Michael Jordan of CS lecture. He is the GOAT.

If you're taking 1302, take Dr. Barnes he is the best. His lectures are very useful and he is also very funny and engaging. The class overall isn't too bad, there are lots of extra credit opportunities, and office hours help. You can get an A as long as you put in the work and don't procrastinate on coding assignments.

This guy's great! I wish he would teach the other cs classes here. Alot of reading assigned before class, the homeworks are great to practice those readings and projects are hard. But he gives ALOT of extra credit because he knows this stuff is hard as hell. Pay attention and he'll set you up for success.

The homework criteria is sometimes really vague and projects are really hard. However, he is a good professor and has great lectures. It's important to start projects early because you usually get 2 weeks or a little less to do them with homework and other stuff going on. Going to class isn't required but it's a must to know what's going on.

Good professor. Does not allow laptops to take notes, so take cotterell instead if you prefer that. Hard projects make sure you start early. Lots of extra credit. Exams are a bit difficult and you don't have much time to take it. Otherwise this is a good class. Hard class but its what is expected as a CS major. Attendance required.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: I love Dr. Barnes. Best professor I've had at UGA, not just in CS. His lectures are mainly writing code as a class so it's not boring PPTs. He likes to have fun with his classes. No final exam or midterm. Just a final project which you get a lot of time for. Always submit your projects early because that's his extra credit and it's a lot of points!
Super nice guy and very helpful! One of the best professors at UGA and I wish he taught other classes besides 1302.
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Best CS Teacher at UGA. Difficult content, but the flipped classroom model and his method of teaching made the material easy to learn and retain. Gives a lot of extra credit, if you keep up with the projects and materials, making over a 100 is doable. Wish Dr. Barnes taught other CSCI classes other than 1302, I would take that class in a heartbeat.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Take Dr. Barnes and don't look back. This class was highly informative and FUN. Lectures are interesting and prepare you for the projects. With that said, this class is hard. If you do not take the readings seriously and pay attention, you will fall behind. Start the projects the DAY they are released and take advantage of the +10 extra credit.
reviews.ratingTags: EXTRA CREDIT--Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Don't get me wrong, this class challenged me in ways that I didn't know were possible. This is the no-joke weed-out class they talk about. With that being said, Dr.Barnes was the best professor I have ever had. He made me excited about my future in computer science. I never missed a lecture just because of his amazing lectures. Take him! Take him!!
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: This man is the best professor I have had so far at UGA. He clearly cares about his students and understands that Computer Science is hard for beginners. He breaks it down for you and if you still don't understand he will make sure that you do after class. It is incredible how good he is. It's obvious he loves his students and his job.

Dr. Barnes teaches you fundamental programming principles from the ground up and will help you address any gaps in your CS knowledge. His course is very challenging, and he will always push you / be tough on you; that being said, he is clear about grading criteria and will assist you with any help you need. I hated 1301, and he makes me like CS!
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Dr. Barnes is the best professor I have had yet at UGA. He is very engaging in his lectures and makes the information easy to understand. The projects in this class will take a significant amount of time, but there is extra credit offered for all of them and it is not difficult to get good grades if you put the work in.

Professor Barnes was one of the best CS teachers I've had so far. He always made the concepts engaging, and really took time to make sure students understood the material. The workload for this class is a lot, so make sure you keep up with assignments and reading, but I really learned a lot from it. Take Professor Barnes!
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--EXTRA CREDIT--Amazing lectures

reviews.comment: Had him for CS 1302 spring semester. He is as good as it gets in my opinion. He gives so much extra credit that it is EASY to get an A in the class. (Extra credit on final project, normal projects, extra credit for in class polls). He does not do quizzes, only tests. Make sure to read the readings he posts on eLC before class and you are chilling.

Dr. Barnes is the best professor I've had! His lectures are very useful, and he is always willing to help students (his office hours are helpful). He provides a lot of extra credit which really help raise your final grade. Although the class is hard, he does a great job teaching the material and making sure everyone understands everything.

Dr. Barnes has been one of my favorite professors at UGA so far and the best CS teacher I've ever had. He genuinely cares about CS and his students and teaches the material in an enjoyable way. Lectures are very helpful because he goes over a lot of examples. He is also very quick in answering questions about assignments and projects in Piazza.

1302 has definitely been one of the hardest classes I have taken and there is a lot of content covered, however, Dr. Barnes is the best professor I've had so far. He explains everything so well in class, so make sure to go to lectures. The projects are hard, but if you go to office hours he will help you and take time to make sure you understand.

Professor Barnes is widely known as the best professor in the Computer Science department at UGA. The coding world gets super complex and so you would think you have to teach yourself a lot, but he perfectly gives you enough new stuff to challenge but not overwhelm you. He is my favorite professor at UGA so far.
He was an incredible professor. Office hours were incredibly helpful and he was always willing to put in the effort so his students could succeed.
He thinks too much of himself and is very reluctant to actually help in office hours

Prof. Barnes is one of the best teachers I've had at Uga. 1302 is a more complex class than 1301 so it is a step up in difficulty, but that doesn't mean the professor is hard. Barnes helps so much and genuinely wants you to see how cool cs is and how to learn how to do it. Just make sure to get a start on projects early or they can kick your butt.
Take Cotterell if you can

Brad is the best professor I have taken in cs so far. He is extremely clear on what he expects from his students and even gives you day by day outline for projects to help you manage your time. We got 7 hws, 4 projects (1 was dropped),3 exams,1 final project (instead of a test) and in class quizzes were bonus points. It's a lot so manage your time.

Great lectures that feel personal with the way he reads the room and tries to make sure people are understanding the content. Almost all assignments feel useful, and although some of the material can be tough, it will feel very rewarding when it all comes together on projects. Useful and productive office hours.

definitely a difficult class, but barnes is a really good lecturer and he provides extra credit and will sometimes extend project deadlines. make sure to start projects early and tests are pretty hard so make sure to really know the content.

He is hands down one of the best professors I've ever had. CSCI1302 is not an easy class whatsoever but Dr. Barnes will make sure that you do well in this class. He does give out a good amount of assignments but it's the only way you'll learn and just trust the process.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Amazing lectures --Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: This class was hard, but Prof Barnes went above and beyond in his lectures and in office hours to help me learn. He understood how hard this class is and offers extra credit.
reviews.ratingTags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Barnes, Cotterell, and LaMarca are the best teachers at UGA. Their 1301 and 1302 classes are a lot of work, but you'll learn so much from them. I highly recommend them.

I cannot say enough good things about Brad Barnes. 1302 is a difficult class but the way he teaches it makes it so much more understandable. His lectures are engaging and with Dr Barnes there really is no such thing as a stupid question. He treats all his students like computer scientists. Although attendance is optional missing class will hurt you

He comes off as charismatic, but it is lined with a lot of condescending behavior. Not to mention borderline bullying behavior after asking for help and ridiculing students.

Chill dude. Not a fan of the tests/exams being weighted so heavily but that's a comp sci thing in general, not just him. You can tell he cares about his students and about making sure we understand the content. My favorite comp sci professor so far.

reviews.comment: 1302 is a very difficult class with the huge workload but Dr. Barnes does an amazing job to keep his lectures focused and useful for students. Amazing pofessor who cares!
