Took him last spring, and ended up withdrawing (for personal reasons) from the class. He was not very engaging, and I constantly saw other people on their phone. Tests & projects b/t him and Dr. Barnes are about the same. I did not personally like him. He simply just wasn't that good at teaching.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Try to avoid him if you can, he's a pain in the butt about everything really. He also is really bad at teaching, pointless and ineffective lectures. He kind of just codes in class and expects everyone to follow along without any type of explanation for what is going on.

Easily one of the worst classes I've taken. CSCI1302 is already a hard enough subject, and Professor Menik has only made it harder and more inefficient. Our lectures were boring, slow, lengthy, and often did not even relate to the day's required reading. Tests were also hard and very different from everything else done in this class.

He gets a lot of hate online but I didn't think he was bad. His lectures consist mostly of example code that can sometimes be copy/pasted into homework/projects and he loves people asking questions. A benefit of 1302 is that if you have trouble with the course, you can ask any of the instructors for help. Overall he's not as bad as people say.

Lecture style is incredibly ineffective. Rarely explains broader topics, only goes over example code during the whole class. I have witnessed multiple times he has struggled to help confused students. In my opinion he is a lazy lecturer. Try to take the course with Barnes or Cotterell. Avoid him if you want to actually learn during lecture.

Just don't. I did well in this class, but you might not because I spent a stupid amount of time on this class because my other classes we comparatively easy. I was not goated last semester, just a no life because of this class.

Nice guy, wants students to do well. Lectures are boring. Relatively easy group project towards the end. Attendance is semi-mandatory. Interesting material and tests are fair. 4 hws, ~8 activities, group project, midterm, final.
Professor stinks at teaching.
Insufferable.
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Try to evade this guy. Lecture is NOT ORGANIZED, makes me more confuse if I ask a question. What I am regretting the most is attending his lecture, and those total polls for bonus credit are like 1-2 percent of the overall grade, so if you're not in a desperate situation, I would recommend putting more time into either projects or homework.

While the class was managable if you completed the readings and took detailed notes. Lectures were unorganized - he didn't have slides and would just typing and asking questions and expected us to follow. He didn't post notes after class and I would be lost if I lost focus for a minute. Not a fan of the lecturing style.

Worst professor I have ever had. I took this class last Spring and his explanations were incredibly roundabout and confusing. I believe he might be knowledgeable about what he is teaching, but he has a very poor way of communicating it. I would recommend that no one takes his class, if possible.

This online class was very easy! Definitely take your time understanding the material and do all your homework. The tests are just like the lectures. The only downside is having to do a group project remotely. He only uses piazza to communicate but he replies fast and he is very helpful.
Took this course in the spring, it was a good course. I'm not sure why it has bad reviews. The class was very engaging, and I learned a lot.
I think I learned a lot from this class. Make sure to keep up with the assignments and din't hesitate to ask questions.
Good course
I enjoyed this class! I think he's a good professor and well informed on the subject.
Good class!
Dr. Menik is an outstanding professor who excels in facilitating class discussions. He is knowledgeable and always willing to help students understand difficult concepts.
Although the professor is new, he is very dedicated to the course. I found the class to be highly engaging and the course overall very satisfactory.

The submissions are all wrong. Dr. Menik was the man! I made sure to read the pre-class readings and understand them so that when I came to class I usually just had one or two questions ready to fire off. Then he'd answer them well! The problem was no one asked questions in class. If you end up with him, ask questions, and he'll help a ton.

Sami Menik is an insecure professor who relies heavily on classroom engagement that he never receives. His lectures are confusing and very roundabout to the topic in question. He will defer questions until "later" only to never actually clarify anything. He is disrespectful to students with real questions but will elaborate on very simple topics.

in general, the class itself is hard. regardless of your prof youre going to have to spend a lot of time doing the readings. menik just make the class even less enjoyable in my opinion. he seems sweet but any other of the professors would be better. on the bright side tho you can go to other professors office hours like barnes or cotterell.
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Not good. Lectures are dry and don't help internalize the material. Questions on slides and in assignments are always hard with several layers of recursion - there is no simple example. No homework meant no grade buffer and no chance to learn on your own terms. Antagonistic course policies. He's friendly and he cares, but the class was a mess.

Please listen to me. Do not take this course with Prof. Menik. The tests are all online for some reason, which wouldn't matter if they weren't IMPOSSIBLE. The projects are insanely time-consuming and he never covers them in class. There is BARELY any extra credit, and they are NOT forgiving with grades. The worst class I've ever taken in my life.
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Difficult exams and group projects, and  Lectures were boring!
I did not like his teaching style. He honestly made me feel stupid at one point in his office hours. Hes a great guy but teaching this class isnt for him.
Makes a intro level course very difficult!
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Not a good professor... His in class lectures don't make much sense and he always tries to make sure people understand, but whenever we ask questions he always gives a more convoluted answer than before.
Avoid!
This was so hard. i tried so hard. im so sad. i taught myself everything. Good Luck Future Students!

I'd advise anyone not to take a course with him (its for your good!). Incredibly difficult to understand anything he says during lectures. His exams and projects are hard for no reason and the material is more difficult compared to Georgia Tech CS.
Just run. If you see his name pop up on the course honestly just wait for the semester to be over. I used to like CS but i've never hated CS as much as I have with him teaching it.
Dr. Menik is by far the worst professor that I have had during my time at UGA. No one knows what is expected of them and there is a consistent F average on the exams
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Avoid at all costs!

To be honest, I think a lot of these reviews are way too harsh. While the class is sometimes difficult and can be frustrating, I don't think it's THAT bad. Be prepared for some exams and projects that aren't really representative of course materials, but if you study and do your work it's not too difficult to receive As and Bs.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Easily the worst teacher I have ever had. He was very condescending towards multiple students in my class. As far as the class, I could have done better than I did had I done more outside of class. The material is hard but you will have to practically do it all yourself to do well. The tests and projects are ridiculously hard for no reason!
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Dr. Menik was the easily the worst professor I have ever had. I attended some office hours in order to ask questions, but he wasn't very receptive to answering. His test and group projects are insanely hard for no reasons. The class average for the test had to be below 55!
This was so hard. i tried so hard. im so sad. i taught myself everything. the exams were always not like the study guides and yeah.

Menik is a good professor for other classes, but not for CS4370 Database Management.This dude does not know what he's even teaching, and tests are hard  seemingly randomly hard for no reason . A friend and I struggle on the group projects. He grades them harsh!
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Test heavy

reviews.comment: This is the hardest course I've ever taken. By the first class, I was lost. I would try to approach him with questions and he would not answer me. Throughout the semester, everything was unclear and I should've just dropped his class in the beginning. Though I give him a bad rating, he does seem to care, AVOID!
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Worse experience and probably the worse professor at the University of Georgia. The professor is nice but his exams and projects are hard for no reason. I wish future students Good Luck!
He's a good guy and very nice but not a good prof. His exams and projects are hard for no reason!
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Dr.Menik is truly the worst professor I've ever had. His exams are so difficult because there impossible.
Awful experience.  Exams and projects are difficult and refused to help or curve the test grades. DO not take this professor, I repeat!
He knows his stuff but he doesn't care for his students. This is a very exam-heavy class. Have to do very well in all the tests to get an ok grade.
This professor is literally the worst professor at UGA. He is the professor that does not teach the material, and make the exams and projects for no reason.
Very difficult professor and a harsh grader!

Terrible professor. The tests are same across all CS classes but the other professors actually teach and prepare you well, this guy doesn't. He has 0 power and no leniency. So basically, you have to be literally dying to get any extension, nothing else will get you one. His lectures are really boring. Course is outdated and not relevant today.
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: If you don't like Dr. Menik, you can go to office hours with other professors/contact them via Piazza. His lectures are fine - sometimes boring. Don't procrastinate, work efficiently, and study for the tests (they're actually not as bad as people say). Sometimes he gives HW answers in the lectures so pay attention
not a good professor. The tests in this class are insanely hard.  AVOID AT ALL COSTS!
His class is a mess. If you value your sanity I would stay away.

Let me start this by saying Sami is a nice man. That being said that doesn't make him a good professor. Sami doesn't have good presenting skill which makes following his lectures difficult. He doesn't prepare his students for his exams well. All of the work he assigns is done in groups (if your group mates don't do well your grade will suffer).

You MUST do the readings every night. I got behind and ended up turning in almost all my homework assignments late which are from the readings and it messed up my grade. Also the test are extremely hard and you just need to be a good CS test taker not just a good programmer. They projects can also boost your grade.

Did not learn anything in this class. It was a waste of my time and money. Exams are difficult and not an accurate reflection of the content we were "taught". For a database class, we barely did any actual SQL practice and application, which absolutely sucks since this an important skill to know for jobs/interviews. Take someone else if you can.

No concept of deadlines, assigned hard and long projects that are BARLEY relevant to database, have to have multiple extensions because he'd only give a week to complete it. If you're in a group with random people, good luck and hope they actually do the work or you're going to fail. He's a cool dude though.

Oh and exams are very hard.
Hes a nice guy, but not a good professor. The tests in this class are insanely hard. I studied my butt off and still got low grades on them. 4 words: avoid at all costs
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment:
Very nice guy, unfortunately falls short. Not a great lecturer, and everything is mandatory. Has 0 leeway on anything, and does not give good feedback on projects and tests. When I asked how I could do better next test he said "You should try studying"(ok thanks!)

Avoid this prof. I am potentially losing Zell bc of this dude.

Nice guy. Very tough grader and doesn't give good feedback when asking questions on HW's or projects. The tests are also very tough and he gives no leeway on changing the exam times or trying to be accommodating. I would definitely take the other CS1302 professors.

RUN AWAY FROM THIS PROFESSOR!! 

He is an absolute nightmare of a professor who has no mercy and 0 power when helping students with extra assignments or redemption. This guy is the most incompetent teacher I have seen over my 3 years in college and while studying at 2 different universities. Take it over the summer online at GSU!
Worst CS professor at UGA, DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS!

Professor Menik is one of my least favorite professors that I have ever taken at UGA. He does not allow you have any family/medical issues that cause you to be unable to take a test at the scheduled time, he will not let you reschedule the test. He is not very understanding or lenient about anything AT ALL!!!
Tests are hard. You need to pay attention and READ. Overall pretty dang hard class.
Too hard, extremely specific exams. Expects too much.

Take the green reviews with a grain of salt. Yes, he's very good at teaching the concepts  but his exams are EXTREMELY hard. He puts more details in the exam than his lectures making it more challenging than it already is. Projects are long!
What a joke, this dude is giving HIMSELF good reviews and perfect ratings.
Do not take this professor, just wait one semester for a better professor.

CS1302 is basically the same no matter who you have. The only thing different is how they lecture, which I didn't find to be super helpful and learned almost everything from the readings outside of class. Dr. Menik does a good job going through topics and gives tips for assignments in class. He is a nice guy I just found it hard to get engaged.

He clearly enjoys this topic. I think the class structure and difficulty are issues. Neither 1301 nor 1302 should be hard. Five projects, three tests. Several homework's. Projects are hard, homework is manageable, and attendance is inconsistent. Lockdown browser tests and class-starting polls.

I was surprised by the amount of negative reviews for Dr. Menik. He really knows his stuff and always gives clear responses when you ask him a question. The lectures can get kind of boring but he tries to make it easy to understand. He's also very kind during office hours.
Not a bad professor ngl, no idea why there are so many 1 star reviews. He's knowledgeable and nice. Work isn't too much, tests aren't bad.
reviews.ratingTags: EXTRA CREDIT--Graded by few things--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Lectures are unhelpful and difficult to sit through. Expect to do hours of self-teaching for projects and tests due to not learning in class. Do not take Prof. Menik
The class was very easy; however, I do not feel that I learned a lot from his lectures. It was hard to focus on them. He was a very nice and humorous professor.
reviews.ratingTags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Overall, a great professor for the class and a very good lecturer. Would recommend for Data Science 1/2.
get ready to be edged, thats all.

Class was a big discussion everyday about the topic at hand. At times the material could be uninteresting, but the discussion was often great. This was his first semester teaching the course, so it will be more rigorous in the future. This semester, there were four homework assignments, a midterm exam, and a final project.
reviews.ratingTags: Amazing lectures --Graded by few things--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Exams are all online with basic ahh lockdown browser  ,doesnt take attendance,  Hw is with some textbook but there is a free verison, This is a free A.

Simple class structure, but being a flipped classroom can make in-depth understanding difficult. Dr. Peng does not teach in class, only works through exercises, so you really need to understand everything before showing up to class. Content can be difficult, it really just depends on how your brain works. Peng is all-around pretty solid though
The best teacher you can take at UGA. Get ready to learn a lot and have fun in the lectures!

He is very funny and teaches using the flipped-classroom method. Attendance is not mandatory AT ALL and 95% of his exam questions are from his quizzes (online and available from day 1) but with different numbers. Overall a great class!

Not much to say other than he was both great at teaching and really funny. Loved his lectures when I went, and good attendance wasn't mandatory for when I couldn't. Go on the review days before exams if you take his class though. They will be your lifesavers.
good class learned a lot

Professor Hao Peng is a wonderful professor! He is genuinely hilarious and caring. Like other students mentioned, Discrete Math is taught in a flipped-classroom style so attendance is not mandatory. All resources for the class are available in elc and the content isn't too difficult. 100% would recommend!
He doesn't require attendance and used flipped classroom. There is a group project at the end. 2 Exams which are online

Good prof who is good at explaining concepts, though he does often go on short tangents.
The actual course does not have much content though, so most lectures are let out 15-30 mins early.
Its also flipped so make sure you read the book even if you go to the lectures

Hao Peng is a funny and easy going guy, often goes on tangents looking random stuff up during class. Flipped classroom, so videos before class are the lecture, class is just extra practice. Realistically if you are a motivated student you dont have to go to class at all to succeed. Hw sometimes long but rest is light, clearly outlined expectations.

Professor is nice but i don't like his teaching style. He posts a bunch of videos that you're expected to watch before class and just solves problems during class without really teaching or explaining the concepts. So if you don't watch the 3-4 vids before class, yeah u won't understand anything in class.
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: He uses a flipped classroom format where you watch his lecture videos (varies from ~10-40 min) before class, and he solves problems in class. He focuses on the math behind machine learning models (which isn't that hard), so the Python coding isn't that intensive. He gives a lot of time for the homework, and he gives a good review before exams.
It was discrete math but it was a flipped classroom. His exams can be quite tricky even though they are online multiple choice with lockdown browser. My GOAT tho

This class is basically an online class, I showed up to class the first two days, and never showed up again. If you are good at self-teaching it's very free. You learn the material yourself at home and he goes over problems in class. If this style doesn't suit you, choose another professor, but if you're good at self-teaching, it's very convenient.

One of my favorite professors I've had at UGA, very nice and willing to help you with problems through being responsive to emails or asking in class. Will probably have to take notes/seek extra help outside of class though. Very test heavy and practically no curve, final is by far the hardest test in the semester. bonus credit so an A is doable

Flipped Classroom format, his videos aren't really that helpful but content isn't that difficult. Drops lowest Homework and quiz grade, and each quiz has two attempts. The tests aren't too hard either, and he gives extra credit at the end of the semester. Overall he seems like a good guy, just isn't the best at explaining the topics in the class.

Show up to class and pay attention and it will be fine. Definitely gets hard towards the end and the final exam is pretty rough, but there is extra credit so take those opportunities. He explains things really well and is easy to reach regarding extra help. One of the best professors in the CS department imo.

If you put in the work, it is an easy class. 6 homework's throughout the semester. Quiz due almost every week with 2 attempts. 3 exams ( 1 is 15%, 2 is 20% and Final is 25%). All exams are online on elc with lockdown + webcam.

You have to teach yourself and then he goes over problems in class. However, these problems are not the same format as the test. There are weekly quizzes which are similar to the test so the best way to study is to practice those! However, he is caring about his students and was accommodating when I had technical difficulties on the final.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Class was ok overall. He doesn't really teach, you are supposed to learn all of the material by yourself. Some of his lecture videos are really confusing, and his in-class exercises are unnecessarily hard. The tests are online, and you get a cheat sheet, but the professor is definitely harder than the other discrete math professors.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Lecture heavy--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Good lecturer, just didn't really like the layout of the class of having to take all the notes outside of class

Honestly has to be my most favorite professor in the entire CS department. His lectures are really good, and he's accessible outside of class. Theory itself hasn't been too bad so far, but the difficult has certainly spiked up since we started Turing machines. Would highly recommend taking him!

Peng is good but this class obviously involves a lot of theory, so things aren't always straightforward at first, but they usually makes sense after doing the classwork. Everything gets worse after Turing machines. Peng posts his slides to eLC, so I'd recommend downloading the slides, so you can listen more actively in class.

Pretty okay class and professor but there are only 3 exams that make up most of the grade along with quizzes and homework. The exams are online and mostly multiple choice but they can be tricky sometimes. If you do all the work and go to class you should be okay.
reviews.ratingTags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Peng is very nice & educated on Theory. Also posts his slides & reviews on eLC. Weekly HW on Grafstate that is only difficult after learning Turing Machines after Exam 2 (allowed to work together on HW but I worked solo since Exam ?s very similar). Final was harder than the 2 exams, but if you study more & always did the HW you should be good.
If you watch all his lecture videos and actively participate in class and do all the work that is expected of you in this course, you should be able to bag an A.

Theory could be hard so be ready to study well. Classwork (work with your group) due every week and isn't that hard. Quick email response, clear instruction, ppt provided. Tests mostly come from your classwork but heavy weighted, as long as you study then you will be fine. Overall, he's nice guy, must take him

Professor Peng can be a little tricky with the projects, but if you take him on Summer, you have two exams and three projects. Do some research for Unix, C++. Other than that you should be good to go. TAs, can be typical TAs. He respond fairly quick, instructions on projects can be a little broad.

I love Peng. His class layout is smart, but different: you watch the lecture online before the class to cover content, then watch its application in person. His tests and assignments are fair. He makes the class easy and interesting, and includes the application of its content. Finally a CS professor with some personality.

Nice guy and knows the material but he is not good at teaching it. Went on many random tangents during lectures. Flipped classroom is not ideal for discrete math imo. I spent so much time doing reading and videos before class but still did not understand the material well. 3 exams, weekly online quizzes (w/ 2 attempts), semiweekly hw.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Decent class. The majority of the learning was done online with practice problems gone over during lectures. Heard from some that he makes the class harder than other profs, but I can't verify that. Attendance is not mandatory at all and you can probably still do fine in the class without ever going at all.

Hao Peng is not a great professor. He's the only discrete professor who does a flipped classroom, so if you are looking for a non-mandatory attendance professor, you found him. Otherwise, if you plan on coming to class and learning from the professor himself, this is not the way to go.

Hao Peng is a phenomenal professor. As someone who did not understand much of Discrete Mathematics content, I was able to perform exceptionally well in this course. Dr. Peng makes it easy to participate and do well. Would recommend to anyone.

Hao peng is really cool. For discrete math he did a flip style classroom. Lecture videos before class and problems in class. I actually liked learning it this way because the in class problems just helped it click better. He also likes going off on random tangents which kinda kept me engaged.

Very good professor, flipped classroom worked well and he makes lectures interesting and engaging due to his likable personality. You dont necessarily have to pay that much attention in class to get good grades if you are a good studier. Only complaints were that the teaching method got repetitive and the second test covers a lot of information.

Clear lectures. Sometimes has funny tangents (ex figuring out what a needle in a hay stack is). Doesn't overcomplicate things. Teaches what you need to know and that's what's on the test. Great for everything, but better in 2670 than 1730. One of best CS professors. Wasn't planning on taking data science, but I will be because he's teaching it.
took F23 and was simple. Projects are moderately difficult...sometimes. Exams were MCQ, and cheat sheets were allowed. Straightforward with test questions and review.

Lectures aren't terribly exciting, easy to zone out. It's flipped so you watch videos and then go to class where he asks conceptual questions. He gives you so much time on homework, some projects can be done in as little as 2-3 hours if you really focus. You get a cheat sheet on both exams. Nice guy, approachable for questions. Good professor!
reviews.ratingTags: Group projects--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: All of his lectures were pre-recorded videos to be watched on ELC, which is not how I like to learn. In class, periods were extremely boring as they consisted of a few practice problems with so much down time in between. Coding projects were hard, but not impossible. Exams allow a cheat sheet, which was a huge life safer.

Dr. Peng never lectured in class and would instead post lecture videos and we would do practice exercises in class. There is one group project at the end of the semester which is not bad. The midterm and final are really easy and he gives a study guide which is basically the same as the exams. Definitely recommend this class as it's an easy A!
reviews.ratingTags: Group projects--Clear grading criteria--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Peng used the flipped classroom model (lectures for homework, assignments during class), and posted the solutions for everything, so attendance was not mandatory. Very easy assignments, and the midterm and final were all math. Take Peng for an easy A!

Very silly and friendly man, with lectures that very directly set you up for homework and exams (hw is just the lecture notes with different data). Lectures can get pretty dry, though, just copy down notes and doodle or something. Also there's a group project+presentation but it's very easy.

He's great, please take him for 1730, he's so silly and I love him. You can tell that he really likes what he teaches. Most of the labs and project can be done by using the code examples he gives you. He is also a pretty good lecturer and made stuff feel easy with his examples. The last two project can also be done with a partner.

I took Peng for 1730, Theory of Computing, and Data Science I. He's chill and gives good lectures, although they can be a little dry (especially for 1730). His workload is super light and his tests are not hard. Attendance is optional; most people skipped class. I really loved him for theory but I'd recommend him for any class.
Love his teaching style of watching videos online and coming in class to do coding activities.
Average lecturer, not bad, not good.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: super easy cs class. 7 labs, 3 projects (2 w/ partner) and 2 exams. for exams, just study the review ppt (mostly) and use the lecture slides he posts to make ur cheat sheet and u will be fine on exams. most of the code for labs/projects are in his example codes

The content is a survey of elementary data science topics, making it too easy for what we have to subsequently do in 4360; that being said, he's such a sweet man that I'm ok with that. Assignments were literally just lecture notes but with different data. A group project, but very easy. He's also chatGPT-friendly.

Dr. Peng was a very reasonable professor and his class had a very defined structure. If you go to class and do the practice problems, you will do well on the quizzes. If you study the quizzes thoroughly, you will do well on the tests. Study for 30-45 minutes a day and follow this cycle and you will surely get an A.

This professor is decent and fair. His TA's were lenient on grading the homework as long as you do things on LATEX. Be prepared to attend lectures since that's the only way you get good practice. His lecture slides are key to getting a decent understanding of the material.

this class was pretty easy but it is graded heavily by tests, so you need to study hard for those. the test comes straight from the review the day before, so make sure to study that mainly. the hws aren't super hard and they're from a textbook that can be found online.

I honestly really liked this class. He made theory managable. However, I did find myself needing Youtube videos to futher understand topics. EasyTheory and Neso Academy will get you through this course. Seriously... just watch videos on things you don't understand and you'll pass. I wouldn't have gotten an A on the first test without the videos.

Dr. Peng is a fair teacher and a funny guy, but I didn't find class to be particularly engaging because it was exclusively working through practice problems. He explains concepts and problems well, but the flipped format made it hard to want to come to class. But if you come to class and work the problems, quizzes and homework are super easy.

3 tests are worth 65% of your grade. I'm normally a really bad test taker but he goes over everything that will be on the test the class before and he lets us use a cheat sheet which has been really helpful. I really enjoy his lectures. I normally find comp sci lectures boring but he works through problems with us using grafstate/drawing.

One of the easiest classes I've taken at UGA. Labs are due every other week, with alot of the answers being in the sample code. He had review sessions for the midterm and final that essentially outlined the test and told the answers. I hadn't stayed for an entire class period until the last week of school, but still ended with an A.
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Peng is a pretty chill and funny professor. The class is easy and honestly kind of fun. There is a group project at the end which was also very fun and interesting. You can get away with skipping, but make sure you do the homework assignments and read his notes.

Mostly reads from ppt slides but does do helpful coding examples during class which is the only reason I went to class, posts everything on elc. Three projects (two of which you can work with a partner on), 7 lab assignments, and two exams (non-cumulative and cheat sheets allowed). Not a difficult class if you just show up and listen occasionally.

Class would've been a lot easier if it didn't get so crammed towards the last few weeks of the semester. Everything is taught from PowerPoint but he provides examples on complex concepts. Allows cheat sheets on exams. Labs and projects were hard for me, but my TA helped a lot.

I get why he has a bad rating, but I thought he slayed for 1730. It is very lecture-heavy content, but you could learn that on your own since attendance isn't mandatory. He is very understanding and helps with labs/projects. The course does have a weird pacing which is rushed/crammed during finals week, but it's doable.

Peng is an average professor, he doesn't teach very well, so expect to learn the content yourself. The class was poorly paced and ended up cramming all the projects toward the end of the semester. He is understanding when it comes to student concerns and will give extensions when needed.

Hao Peng is really a blank slate for you to be a student on. For 2610, to really understand anything, you need to do practice problems yourself. He walks you through every step, and attending class is the only way to get solutions. HWs are just slightly modified in-class problems, and he let me turn in late HW for full credit.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Online Savvy

reviews.comment: Not sure why Peng gets such a bad wrap, I found the class to be quite manageable. Though just a little bit disorganized, Peng explains the material well and provides you plenty of time and resources to finish assignments. He does not go overboard with the difficulty, but it is a fair challenge. Overall imo a very solid pick for 2670.
reviews.ratingTags: Clear grading criteria--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Peng did the bare minimum as a prof. Like youll get through the class, but i never felt like Peng cares about his students. For the first 2 months of class he was late.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: what are he talking about on the class He was sometimes a few minutes late, and he taught with only a text editor. And he jump so fast that the process of some problems was not even complete

Extremely unorganized lectures where he does nothing but go on tangents and murmurs to himself. The pacing is terrible where you have labs and projects due on the same night. There wasn't any other professor teaching 1730 when I took it but I wish there was.

2 exams made up more than 50 percent of your grade. Horrible pacing in the class, all 3 projects were in the second half of the semester. Most of the class was Peng reading off slides which were very boring and hard to pay attention to. Exams were fairly difficult, but the labs and projects were not too bad imo

Dr. Peng made this class a lot boring. The concepts taught in this class are interesting and beneficial for future, but Dr. Peng just reads them from the slides and takes no effort in explaining them. I would not recommend taking any class with him if you want to learn anything from the class. It is an easy A class but you would learn nothing.

Peng is a great guy and a decent professor. Class is taught through PowerPoints and he uses examples of certain concepts on Odin. If you have questions or are confused about a topic meeting with him should be your top priority. The lectures can be slow and you need to put the work in outside of class and if you want an A.
reviews.ratingTags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: I liked Dr. Peng less and less as the semester went on. The pacing in the course isn't great. We get used to a schedule of 1 lab every 2 weeks and then suddenly we have a lab due the same day as a big project and the dates overlap so you're suddenly flooded with work near the end. Sometimes he's funny and relatable but he just reads off slides.
reviews.ratingTags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Graded by few things--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Poorly paced class - most of the work was put towards the last month or two of class, instead of being evenly spread out. Lectures just consist of him reading off slides. Test questions were sometimes confusingly worded, but that's just the usual for CS exams. Not many projects however, and he gives you the option to work with a partner sometimes.

Class is solely him monotonously reading notes, never attempting to explain concepts further than the notes do. While the only assignments are homework, they teach things that don't happen in the real world, giving students false impressions of how machine learning works. You would receive more benefit from a text-to-speech engine and a textbook.

Great professor. He explains things in detail and makes sure you understand the materials by providing more examples. The course requires some efforts, but he is not a rough grader. Definitely recommend.

Knows alot of the material in depth but simply lacks the ability to effectively teach the material. He rushes through the material and doesn't provide adequate explanations to new concepts. Be ready to just relearn everything from class on youtube/chegg cuz he will make you more confused then when you first came into class.

Ive taken classes with Dr. Peng multiple times and hes always a great choice! Hes very willing to answer questions and help when you ask for it. His tests are on the difficult side(I think) but totally doable if you study and have a good grasp of the concepts. Not an easy A professor, but if you put in the necessary effort its a great class.

Dr. Peng is a good professor at explaining the topic itself, and reinforces the concepts by going through example problems. The homework is not too difficult if you pay attention in class. The weekly quizzes also follow the lecture, so they aren't bad at all. His tests focus mostly on quiz concepts rather than the homework. Overall, a solid choice

I have no idea what some of the other reviews are talking about. He is genuinely one of the most caring teachers I have ever had, and made this class suck a lot less than it should have. he kind of feels like your cool and wise older brother at times.

Peng is one of the most amazing teachers I have ever had. Our whole class loves his character. The class is hard, and the work is extensive, but it's discrete math. It's not supposed to be easy. He genuinely cares about each student's individual success and he gives you room to succeed. If you reach out to him, he will do whatever it takes to help.
reviews.ratingTags: Respected--Accessible outside class--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: Professor Peng is knowledgeable but also understands that students probably don't have an extensive background in data science. The class covers basic models and the math behind them and asks you to apply them through the homework and group project. If you like data science, you'll like Dr. Peng's class.

Discrete math is about as interesting as watching paint dry. Peng is a decent professor who is knowledgeable. However, his lectures are dry and he does not make a good effort at ensuring you understand the material. Also if you miss one part of a question, you get the whole question wrong. Unnecessary stress and bad grading criteria. :(

For someone with 0 prior programming experience, this was a tough class. The projects and labs were fine & really were one of the biggest sources of learning/understanding of concepts for me. However, Professor Pengs tests were so so difficult & meant to trip you up. The class itself wasnt bad, but his tests can be the difference between an A & B

Avoid this class at all costs if you are a beginner to programming. The labs and projects are straightforward, but tests are extremely hard. His tests ask questions about the most unimportant details about programming and are meant to trip you up. His lectures are also not the best, and he is bad at explaining key concepts.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: I did not enjoy Professor Peng. The class was so lackadaisical but then the tests were very hard. Briefly reviewed reading from the night before and then gave mandatory group worksheets. Physical papers but they had to be turned in online to ELC. Doesn't give clear explanations for how he solved a problem or why he did something a certain way.

Dr. Peng gave us a good combination of lecture and classwork. The material is fairly simple, and as long as you show up to class or read the material you should be alright. I do recommend going to class, however, the classwork can be done on your own time. Don't forget to turn in classwork and homework because it is a decent part of your grade.
Professor does not teach one bit. He reads off the lecture slides and sometimes rushes the lecture. Would not recommend taking him.
He just reads off of the slides that are poorly made and hard to understand. Then throws a worksheet at u to figure out yourself.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Group projects

reviews.comment: Mr. Peng was a nice guy and nothing against him- but this class is impossible if you have no programming background, unless you literally dedicate several hours each day to practice. Workload is ridiculous for a beginner class. Dropped&took it at another school and made an A- if you just need this one course for your major I suggest doing the same.
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Hao Peng is very knowledgeable but unfortunately a pretty bad teacher. He will read the slides for you for an hour and then apply tests on stuff 3x harder than what you see in class. Labs/projects are fair and straightforward, the main issue are the tests which require plenty of studying and preparation due to the professors lack of teaching skill
reviews.ratingTags: LECTURE HEAVY--Tough Grader--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: I had him for 2610 and also for 1730, and I enjoyed both classes. He cares that you understand the material, and he's interesting and funny enough to keep you awake during lecture. Obviously a pretty smart person.
Nah, man... nah

This is the worst professor I've ever had. If you have him for CS1301 then get ready to learn everything on your own because he's just so unhelpful. There is not even any motivation to go to class because he skims over slides and gives a tough exercise right after. If you have no programming experience, I urge you to avoid this man at all costs.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--LOTS OF HOMEWORK

reviews.comment: Took this class before at a previous college and was still completely lost. Once past introductory slides, class becomes extremely difficult, mostly due to his strange lectures that are hard to understand/follow. Isn't the most respectful/genuine professor either. Don't collaborate , they will accuse you of cheating and knock a letter grade off.

By far the worst professor I have ever had, he is completely disorganized, very slow during lectures, and if you take his class you will be very behind compared to many of the other CS classes. He does not prepare you well at all for the tests especially because he does not write the tests. The class is already hard and he makes it even worse.
reviews.ratingTags: Skip class? You won't pass.--LOTS OF HOMEWORK--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: This class requires you give it some time before the tests. IF you start studying though about a week before the test a few hours a day you will be fine. Just do book problems. THEY WILL BE THE TEST QUESTIONS.

Hao Peng is a great guy. He's a good teacher for a fairly difficult subject and it helps when he slows down to reexplain something. If you're confused about some topics, he does work with you outside of class to understand it better. He's a caring person who wants to see all of his students succeed.

Great teacher for discrete. Tough subject, but he explains it clearly and works through every problem you have a question on. Class is interactive and he always considers what you have to say. He is a little of a push over though which is why I gave him a 4/5 rather than a 5/5. He can get distracted by class questions and not get to new topics fast

Professor Peng is an extremely smart professor and you can definitely tell during class. He teaches the information in the best way possible, but Discrete Math as a subject is simply difficult to learn. It is a lot of random topics thrown at you in a random order. Do not go into this class and expect it to be easy, it is not. Cheat sheets for tests
reviews.ratingTags: Clear grading criteria--TEST HEAVY--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Might be the goat of the CS department. Had him for ds and algo and he was goated for both. The content is difficult at times, but half of it is from ds and the other half is straightforward if you attend his lectures. might not have gotten a "free A" but I can confidently say I know the material, which imo, is what's worth it. Thank you Dr. Meena!

Dr. Meena is a FANTASTIC professor for algorithms. He always ensures you understand exactly what he wants. The first half of algorithms is very hard, the second half is not bad at all. The homework are lengthy, but they are exactly like the quizzes and tests, so they were great practice. I would 100% recommend Dr. Meena for algorithms.
reviews.ratingTags: Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Great professor for algorithms. Is very clear on what will be on the test and quizzes, and questions are always from material covered in lectures. Will record his lectures and post them online, which is great for reviewing all the content. HW was reasonable but somewhat time-consuming, so make sure to start on it early. Would recommend overall.

Meena is a great professor. He always explains the concepts of the class clearly. The class is graded by few things so you need to practice, but he is accessible after class and in office hours, so he makes getting help easier. No real complaints about the professor or teaching style, just practice and start projects early.
reviews.ratingTags: Caring--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Not sure why comments are bashing him harshly, he just takes attendance serious. It's hard to understand him sometimes, but he's my fav CS prof yet, and he explains material well. Tests are EXACTLY off lectures. He crammed graphs in the end and made final too early, but that's because the entire class voted for an early final right before the break

The class started hard but got much easier as the semester progressed. Going to class is vital since he drops a lot of hints for quizzes. You get a 3 page cheat sheet for each quiz and test. Not terribly great at explaining things clearly but he goes over all you need to know for assesments. Homework problems translate almost directly to assesments
Generally fine class for most of the semester, got very rushed in the final couple of weeks though (and not all of the content was covered)

Very touchy about attendance. Will call you out and make a scene if you leave early. One time he forgot that a student had already asked permission to leave early and got mad at him. I could never understand anything he was saying because he doesn't speak clearly and mumbles a lot, and his handwriting on the board is also a mess. Very disorganized.

Sitting through Meena's lectures makes me feel embarrassed to be a UGA student. I've never had a professor who understands his own content so poorly. He cannot get through his own examples, making it impossible to follow along and creating many awkward moments in every lecture. I'll definitely get an A, but would never take him again.

3 people out of 150 got the most valuable question on the midterm correct and he still wouldn't drop it. Statistically speaking, that should inform a professor that there's something wrong with how the material is being taught. He takes attendance almost every class, make sure you're on-time and don't try to leave early (unless he knows why before)

He brags about reporting students for academic dishonesty from the very first lecture. He appears to get an ego boost from giving his students difficult work and poor quality teaching and then reporting their assignments if they look even slightly similar. I was recommended to take his class because the other 2720 professors are supposedly worse...
reviews.ratingTags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Meena cares about teaching but his lectures are so hard to follow at times. HWs are much harder than quizzes/exams. Quizzes & exams are very fair. I had a 92 the whole time & I only got a B- bc I got CLEARED by the final bc he tested us before finals week & I had a bunch of other stuff going on... So I would recommend studying a lot for exams..

His quizzes/tests were honestly super easy. It's the assignments/homework that made the class pretty tough. His lectures are super hard to follow, which is sad cause you can really tell he does care about his students performance. Just go over slides prior to tests and quizzes and you'll be fine. Also get used to “guys” he REALLY loves that word
The attendance rules were strict, the two exams were hard, and I got a lower grade than expected. But honestly, I can't even be mad. He generally teaches well and the grading is fair.

Had him for both data structures and algorithms. Avoid for algorithms because his lecture make no sense. The issue is that Meena thinks he is being helpful to students when he's really not. He jumps around all over the place when teaching and his writings never make any sense when note-taking. His tests/quizzes suck.

Used to love Computer Science until I took this fellow for Data Structures. I learnt more by reading the slides than trying to understand his speech. Unfortunately, Data Structures is like a max heap of a garbage pile and this guy is at the tippity top of the trash after the ReHeap up algorithm. INSANE ego problem do not be testing him.
reviews.ratingTags: Lecture heavy--Graded by few things--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: We have 6 homework assignments, 5 quizzes, a midterm, and a final. One homework after taking the final. The TA lowers points for commas, but one grades well. Professor, talk about class in the first 15-20 minutes of your quizzes. Then you must complete it quickly. Attendance: 5%. He is alright because the others are worse.

Quizzes/Exams are easy if you study correctly, lectures go way more in depth than you actually need. Lots of content, but Meena is understand of this and does his best to spread out workload. Get ahead on homeworks and work on them as he is going over stuff rather than all at once at the end. Focus on being able to apply the algs, not the proofs.
Horrible class

there are 2 exams with 6 hws and 5 quizzes. attendance is mandatory but you should be there since the questions from hws, quizzes, and exams come from his lectures, especially the examples he provides. really good for algo because he is helpful in making sure all students understand since algo is really difficult.

Pretty good professor. 4 HW's, 4 programming assignments, 5 quizzes, 1 midterm, and 1 final. Extra credit for submitting everything on time, attendance is required (4 misses allowed). Latter half of course content is easier than first. Meena has an accent but it's understandable. Quizzes and test ask very specific questions ask for time complexity.
reviews.ratingTags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Meena is overall a pretty good professor for 2720. Homework and Projects should be easy 100's if you do them correctly. Attendance is mandatory, but he does not care if you pay attention. Although his lectures are hard to follow, he is decently helpful outside of class if you need help. If you properly study for quizzes and tests, you'll do fine.

Really clear lectures and explanation of topics. Really pay attention to his examples, which is often how you will have to do the homework problems and in turn the exams. The content is tedious, yet Meena really tries to help students understand. His lectures about proofs are a little drawn out and boring but that's just proofs.

Meena is all over the place with his lectures, but he usually never gives you topics on tests you don't learn in class which is better than most. Homework and coding assignments are not hard, just time consuming. Tests are straightforward with some studying. I liked him enough to choose him for algorithms. The notes he posts are unorganized.
I really liked Meena's teaching style. He can be strict but I appreciate it as it helped me personally a lot. He is very fair and really does care about his students.

Definitely not as great as the 1302 professors but 100% an A is attainable in his class. You can work with a partner on coding assignments and he gave extra credit at the end of the semester which saved me. Lectures are mandatory but it is a bit difficult to follow along/understand him, and there's no one else you'd rather take this class with.

6-question, parts A through G type homework in Latex. Tests with imperfect English that make them ambiguous and tough to answer in the precise way he wants. Don't bother asking him for help either. Overall, he's definitely a smart guy but there's zero reason to take him over other professors who are good at the material AND teaching it.

Projects disorganized, broken-English slides alongside his lectures, but doesn't use them as he darts around the whiteboard drawing (and often messing up) formulas with handwriting that looks like the galactic alphabet. He prides himself in exams that test for memorization of unimportant details he skims past instead of understanding concepts.

good at teaching? maybe. only grades by quizzes/tests and homework that doesn't require any coding. if you're struggling in this class, don't expect any help from him outside of the lectures, he will just tell you to get good and won't care if you pass or not
reviews.ratingTags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I read a comment saying he's better than some worse professors, and I see why. Although he might understand the course material, his explanations are poor. Hopefully, he doesn't teach with Professor Arpinar again, as Arpinar is the worst. They deduct 3 points for attendance, which is abusive, when you still have to teach yourself outside of class.

Very smart and funny guy! Amazing lectures- lots of material packed into lectures, but if you listen, you will do well. Preps you for quizzes/exams and is very fair. He makes difficult material doable! You can tell he knows what he is talking about and puts effort into making sure students understand. Would take him again!

Meena is a great professor, always goes over concepts thoroughly, will tell you exactly what you need to know, and encourages questions. Lots of extra credit and partner projects, would take him again.

He speedruns everything. Material is not that hard but he covers too much stuff in a single class. No extra credits , no curve. 5/5 is crazy, someone buys him some bots to give him a fake 5/5 rating. The only 5/5 in the whole department is the 1302 Brad and mike duo, if u know u know.

Ok guys, so, Sachin Meena is a very good professor. If you attend all his lectures (attendance is graded so you should!) you will have all the understanding necessary to excel in this course. He can be funny and does ample examples on the board, so you are always up to speed. Projects are difficult but doable and extremely useful, right guys.

Take this guy for Data Structures. His lectures can sometimes be long and a little boring but you won't have any trouble on quizzes and Exams if you show up to class. Does a crazy amount of extra credit at the end of the semester.
Sometimes confusing to talk to but very caring and understanding of extentuating circumstances. Liked him as a professor a lot.

He is overrated because the other professors who teach that course are so bad. I recommend taking his data structures and then his algorithm. He refers to his previous course lectures a lot, and do read the textbooks because he sometimes doesn't make sense. He tries and cares for his students, though.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: He is one of the best CS professors at UGA. If a student does not understand a concept in class, he makes time to ensure that they understand it. The way he teaches requires you to be an active listener but, he is not that hard to follow. There are a good amount of homework and quizzes, which are very good for preparing you for the exams.

Best professor for data structures and algorithms, but only because the others are worse. His notes on the board are very difficult to follow, but I was usually able to figure it with some youtube to supplement. Programming assignments were tedious, but not hard. Pretty strong accent. Nit picky questions on quizes/tests. Not bad, but not the best.
Prof. Meena obviously knows the material very well and provides lots of opportunities for extra credit, pay attention in class and you'll do well, otherwise this class can be difficult.

Took him for 4470 &2270. Take him for both if you can. In 4470, he allows a 3 page cheat sheet & quizzes are not difficult. The averages on all tests have been a B. He is very considerate.Attendance is taken almost everyday. Just take Meena for this class. Most people who take other professors for this class struggle. With Meena, its light work.

Goes over topics well in class and the slides, hws, and projects are very helpful for quizzes/exams. Quizzes and exams are easy as long as you study a bit and make sure you understand the material. Lectures can be a bit boring and the slides, assignments, and hws have really everything you need. Easy with deadlines and gives info on quizzes/exams.

Meena is great. His lectures can be a bit boring sometimes, but he makes sure that you understand the concepts and he gives extra credit for a lot of things. Also, attendance is mandatory. It can be difficult to understand him sometimes, but he does really care and wants you to see you succeed. Right guys?

Had him for Fall 2023. It was his first time teaching Algorithms, so his teaching method is still under development. Homework and quizzes naturally difficult due to the nature of the course; he allows cheat sheets for the quizzes and tests. Textbook highly recommended since practice problems help tremendously. Attendance taken for grade.
reviews.ratingTags: Gives good feedback--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: attendance is mandatory, but gives extra credit for perfect attendance and submitting assignments on time as well as submitting hws online. quizzes are easy, but midterm and final are hard, so study really well for those. overall, he's a very good lecturer and good at explaining concepts.

One of the best CS professors in the department. He offers lots of extra credit including extra credit for attending class. Very understanding and knowledgable. Exams and quizzes are difficult and studying is required so make sure to attend lectures to grasp the concepts. Meena is very knowledgable and knows it can be hard so helps where he can!
Attendance was required. Lots of extra credit if you go to class/do his assignments. Difficult midterm and final exam. Lectures were boring.

Dr. Meena is an amazing professor who genuinely cares about his students. He offers a lot of extra credit (perfect attendance, doing course evaluations, not submitting projects late, etc). You must go to class and pay attention in lectures to do well on the quizzes/tests otherwise they can be hard. Overall, he's a great teacher and I recommend him!

Meena is a good professor for other classes, but not for algorithms. This dude does not know what he's even teaching, and quizzes and tests are arbitrarily graded with seemingly random scores being given by the TA. A friend and I had the same answers on the multiple choice midterm, but I got 5 extra points than him.

Can do great on programming assignments, but if you don't study for the exams, you will not do great. Attendance is extra credit (go to every class and get the credit, miss one, don't get credit) Homework based on concepts and programming assignments based on structures.

Professor Meena is an amazing instructor. He genuinely cares about his students and he is incredibly lenient. I honestly enjoyed his class and he is always willing to help his students. Midterm and final were pretty easy and overall the assignments were not hard. Mr. Meena is the best choice for Data Structures.

Attendance is basically mandatory, but it is hard class if you don't show up. If you show up to all lectures and take notes you will make an A. He also gives extra credit for showing up and not turning things in late. He also cares about turning you into a better student. Great teacher.

He's a good lecturer and gives a light workload. He kept a good pace and class was pretty engaging. His tests and quizzes weren't easy but the homework prepares you well for them. After taking Discrete with him, I also took his Data Structures class and didn't regret it.

Show up to class, he takes random attendance that is optional but adds pts to a test iirc (maybe midterm or lowest?) He says what content will be tested on in class. Data structures swapped from being taught in C++ -> Java, and it shows, the material & examples he goes over is in C++ syntax and even one of the exam problems was in C++...

A lot of extra credit and genuinely wants you to pass/learn. Quizzes/Exams focus on what he said in lectures. Coding projects+hw are easy. He's kinda funny too, but lectures were boring. I don't like how his course is structured and the last chapter (which is the most important) is rushed. EZ A.

Class is boring and difficult to some, but this man is the GOAT of the CS department, explains everything well if you go to class. tests and quizzes are simple in my opinion as he gives you the topics for each exam. Meena is the best CS professor you can get so take him
Good teacher, material is dry but he is clear about what will be on quizzes/tests, covers everything in class

If you attend class you will do good. He gives a lot of EC at the end of the semester and gives EC for attendance. He is understanding of deadlines and frequently gives extensions. He explains topics very well in lecture also. One of the better CS professors.

Extra cred opps, like attendance, typing up homework, and turning in every project on time. Lets you work on projects with a partner, super accessible outside of class/piazza. He extended like three of our projects since we just communicated that we needed the extra time. Deliberately tells you whats on the quiz in class. Overall a great prof.

Meena seems to actually care about his students and lectures, also just a likable guy. He teaches 2720 very math-heavy, but provides lots of resources. He was sympathetic and often worked with us on tight due dates. Only complaint is that the difficulty of the quizzes and tests is really crazy sometimes. Projects are manageable. Be ready to work.

I had Meena in Fall 2022. He was a decent lecturer that pulled content from the textbook, and most classes are presentation/powerpoint heavy. The textbook is not required since he posts the lecture slides, but it is highly recommended for success in that class. He also took random attendance around 5 times applied as extra credit. Overall good.
reviews.ratingTags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Prof Meena was a good professor and I would recommend him for any class he teaches. His lectures weren't the best but he is very clear on what will be on the quizzes/exams. Also HW is easy and a large portion of your grade. I got an A- with minimal effort.

Gives extra credit on random days for attending class and for typing your homework. Kind of fast paced but he does care for his students and is understanding. Allows a cheat sheet for quizzes and exams. HW every 2 weeks with a quiz about every 3 weeks. Just go to class and take good notes and do well on the exams and you should be fine.
reviews.ratingTags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Very lecture heavy, many quiz and test questions come directly from the lecture, not PowerPoints or book. I struggled to pay attention in class, so I relied on the book. After implementing four data structures, understanding becomes more prioritized than knowing how to implement the remaining data structures. Takes random attendance for EC.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Optional attendance, and one homework assignment every two weeks made this class not too bad, but he doesn't give the entire class time for our quizzes,which made them harder to complete.

Has an accent which made him a little difficult to understand but he offered extra points for typing your homework and there's a youtube playlist that follows his class well so as long as you watch a couple of those videos whenever you're lost you'll pass his class easily

-Gives quite a bit of extra credit
-clearly says in class what will be on the exams
-quizzes and tests are difficult so prepare well
-TA's are kind of useless
-attendance is not mandatory but recommended
-Assignments can be challenging at times but get easier as you go along
reviews.ratingTags: EXTRA CREDIT--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Meena is a great teacher, but his organization and lecture speed is a bit too unorganized. However, he does care for his students. He tells his students whats going to be on exams Clearly, the other students have not been going to lecture because even though the notes are in c++, he explains how to do it in Java. Go to class and write notes!

Overall, Dr. Meena was okay. Projects, homework, and quizzes often had unclear or ambiguous wording. He gave extra credit if you showed up to class, and gave free points for not turning in late work. The course PowerPoints and textbook use C++ code, but he approaches all of the content in a language-agnostic way.

I would agree with the majority of the reviews. We unfortunately are the first class to go through the java version of data structure and I just honestly hated that he did "3 WEEKS" of "math" review. He's not terrible but if you don't understand what he's doing on the board you will have to use outside resources.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: this class was structured terribly. all the notes and textbook are in c++ yet the assignments are all in java. he spent so long on big-O and linked lists that now we have to cram the rest of the content in just over a month, on top of that hes forcing a 1 week early final on us due to him saying the morning class preferred it. overall just not good

Professor Meena is an overall good professor with good intentions. If you show up to class and pay attention, should be a breeze for CS students. Assignments can be pretty lengthy so starting ahead of time might be the only word of advice. Other then that, he wants you to pass so tests and quizzes are straight from lecture.

He isnt a terrible professor but giving out good grades does not mean he is an excellent professor. Spends half the semester on linked list and Big O (which we already covered in another course) and then we are cramming to fit in all the other important material for the rest of the semester. This class just feels useless and you wont really learn.
reviews.ratingTags: EXTRA CREDIT--Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Most of the class comes from his lectures where he goes over slides, but I don't have a problem with that as he's educated and knows what he's talking about. The projects allow you to have partners and aren't hard. He also was willing to give our class extensions twice due to very fair and valid reasons which I have a lot of respect for.

Lectures are hard to understand sometimes due to his thick accent, but he gives out plenty of extra credit just for showing up, and for turning in all (five) coding assignments on time. Exams were closed note, but not that hard or annoying. Generally a better pick than most other professors in the CS department.

Meena's a good prof, and he's really good about answering questions in detail. You get a lot of extra credit for attendance and all his assignments are pretty easy. Uploads slides and goes off them for class, will also give you quiz and test hints in class so attend and you're set. He's also super understanding and helps as much as possible

I thought his lectures were confusing and tbh just ended up teaching myself most of the material. I recommend getting the textbook bc a lot of the homework problems came straight from there. However he was a super nice and understanding guy - you got extra credit from going to class and typing your homework. Also could use a cheat sheet on quizzes

The projects are tedious but youre allowed to work in groups of 2, so definitely use that if you can. Quizzes and tests are super reflective of lecture and will be easy if you go to class and listen. Dr. Meena explains the material really well and is very good at answering whatever questions you may have! I would definitely take him again

Midterm: 25% - Final 25% - Quizzes & HW: 20% - Projects: 30% Decent lectures. Constantly assigning projects. Most everything you need to know is in his lecture slides which he posts to ELC. Honestly a very average class.

I took this class during a covid semester so everything was online. Dr. Meena explains things clearly and really turns most concepts in the class into straight forward memorization. Grading is relatively lax for most of the class, although the final group project (20% of your grade) is graded pretty tough by comparison.

(This Was during a Covid Semester) overall his zoom class was too hard to pay attention to, as he just read off the slides and has a thick accent with a terrible mic making it hard to hear. Not to mention it was a camera pointed at the problems making it really hard to see. The class itself would've been super easy if it was easier to pay attention

Dr. Meena is a great professor. Covered all material during lectures so projects are pretty easy and you can tell he cares about his students' education. The only criticism is that, for being a Data Structures class, it can feel a little too easy, at times. But, you can always use external resources for the extra challenge.
reviews.ratingTags: Gives good feedback--Accessible outside class--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Dr Meena is actually one of the most caring CS professors at UGA. His lectures cover all content and you can easily not read the book. His office hours are so useful so go to them! The coding assignments were self-explanatory and are not meant to trick you. Quizzes and exams wording can be confusing but nothing obscure isever tested on exams&quizze
reviews.ratingTags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Don't think you can learn a lot from his class because it's always too easy. He turns coding into memorization. What he covered would be exactly on the tests/quizzes. He's a very very nice guy by the way. If you screw up, go to his office hours and you'll be fine.
reviews.ratingTags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: The lectures were very helpful as the assignments are almost just copy/pasting from the slides/textbook. The lectures sometimes lacked information and dragged on but they're important because they go over the nuances that are on quizes/exams. The class is very easy but it gets boring learning about one author's (at times, messy) implementations.

Prof Meena's class was honestly pretty easy. The assignments he assigned were well in advance and you could work with a partner (like to divi up the work) on almost everything. The tests weren't too bad and overall he's a good guy. You don't have to pay attention that much, but I'd go to class if you could.

Funny guy. I used the textbook because I wanted to but his lectures pretty much always covered EXACT things on quizzes/tests. He was always very explicit about what would be on assignments. Projects were the usual in UGA CS.
reviews.ratingTags: Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Meena is the best prof I've had for any of my csci courses! He's extremely helpful during office hours and everything comes basically straight from his slides. I really enjoyed his teaching and even though the material can be boring, he doesn't try to make things more difficult than they are. He's funny and nice!
He really cares about his students. A lot of the material is covered in the lecture and if you take the time to understand the concepts he talks about in class, Data structures is really simplified.

Dr. Meena is a very nice and caring professor. Even though the lectures can get boring over time, all the material for the quizzes and exams come straight from the lectures. He's very fair about the exams and projects. If you pay attention to the lectures, you can get an A.

He's not an awful teacher but not great. He takes attendance every once in a while when a lot of people aren't there and that's used for extra credit. There's also a bonus on most of the projects, and if your project average is above 100 then your extra points can go into another category. The biggest problem is that every category is 25%.

Guy's super nice and very lenient on grading, given that you show up to class and participate. The only gripe I have with his class is that EVERYTHING is worth 25% of your grade. So if you get 100's on everything, but get a 75% average on quizzes? You're getting a B. It's so stupid and I hope he changes it. Otherwise a good teacher.
reviews.ratingTags: BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--Clear grading criteria--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: You are not obligated to come to class or buy the book, but you will suffer from it. There is a lot of memorization of terms and tree algorithms. Speaking of which, you are guaranteed a C at worst if you just read the slides over and over. The homework is time consuming, but really easy. I cannot imagine anyone failing this unless they try to.

Meena's class was basically measured on how well you could memorize slides. Attendance isn't mandatory but going to class is very helpful because he takes a lot of quiz questions from points he went over in class (that aren't even in the slides). He also gave extra credit based off of attendance once. Projects are straightforward and have bonuses.

Pretty decent professor; the textbook is optional but get it because it helps a lot for projects + quizzes. Read the slides 10 times before a quiz and you should be good. Projects are really easy. Overall this class didn't require a ton of effort, and extra credit on projects + for coming to class. It's almost impossible to fail.
reviews.ratingTags: Clear grading criteria--GRADED BY FEW THINGS--EXTRA CREDIT

reviews.comment: Meena knows the stuff, but he has a tendency to read exactly what is on the slides. Quizzes are extremely specific, and relies more on how well you can memorize the slides rather than applicable knowledge. Projects are another story, they are fairly lenient as long as you do exactly what is asked, which isn't too difficult. Mostly self-study.
reviews.ratingTags: Tough Grader--BEWARE OF POP QUIZZES--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: meena is a nice dude but he wasnt a great lecturer and his tests/quizzes were very textbook specific. the class is easy if u can memorize ppt slides, but beyond that nothing is exactly intuitive and tests were vaguely/poorly written. i think meena does have potential to be a great professor, however, and his tests/quizzes improved over the semester
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--LECTURE HEAVY

reviews.comment: Great Professor. Personally feel like he understands all the material. Sometimes it might be hard to explain some concepts that would require drawings, but overall, everyone will do fine if you just listen and go through the powerpoints. Assignments are straight forward, but could be more challenging. Office hours are extremely helpful. 6/5

Sachin Meena is a decent professor. He's very nice. He is not the best at conveying information, though. The quality of the slides is fairly low and the coding projects do very little in the way of teaching the content. He is not a bad professor, but there are better options. Online materials will be very helpful in supplementing the material.

Great professor. This was his first semester teaching Data Structs, so it can only get better from here on out. We had a midterm and a final, a few quizzes and 4 programming assignments. All were not bad and the book really helped with the assignments. I take my final in 2 days and expect to get a B or an A- in the class. Would recommend.
reviews.ratingTags: Gives good feedback--ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS--GRADED BY FEW THINGS

reviews.comment: Bismillah, do not take. Thank allah she is retiring.

Yeah it was a tough class with a ton of homework and quizzes, though I feel like the only way to learn algorithms is to do a bunch of problems yourself. All the assignments helped prepare me to pass the tests. Youll easily pass if you keep up with the boatload of assignments and videos, otherwise youre doomed.
AVOID HER AT ALL COSTS. Uses a flipped class and doesn't really teach you. Classes consist of just worksheets and she doesn't teach in person.

Flipped classroom so all the learning is done on your own through her videos recorded 4 years ago. Random groups every 2 weeks; in your group you work on 2 worksheets a week that are difficult and long, the videos barely prepare you for them. Lots of content to study on tests, but she does grade very leniently on them. Try not to take her
