There's a ridiculous number of assignments, homework, and quizzes. We had 27 worksheets, 4 homework, and 18 quizzes. It will take a LOT of your time. Also, hearing from other people that's taken the same course with different professor, their midterms and final exams are way easier than Funk's... If you can, I highly suggest different professor

Flipped classroom setting and gives an absurd amount of work during group work and homework. Quizzes were very doable.  Uses zoom for "lectures" and takes attendance as well. If you are taking this during summer, do not, especially if you are taking other classes besides this one, it is a very big time committment.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Absurd amounts of busy work; group worksheets due EVERY day where your group may or may not participate. The content covered in the slides/videos are basic, and then worksheet/HW questions are very difficult and cover content which you would have no way of knowing(and graded for accuracy). Slow grading, and mistakes. She does not reply to emails.
Shelby Funk although is a very nice woman, she does not teach. She uses a reverse classroom method and during lec
This professor is a very strict grader who is slow to respond to emails.
She is a very tough grader and responds very slowly to emails, in fact she cherry picks the emails she responds to.
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--So many papers

reviews.comment: SO BAD! A lot of your grade comes from the group work and if you get stuck with a bad group it is unfair that she doesn't ever try giving you credit. She has not responded to one email this whole semester. She grades very harsh and so tedious about everything.

Dr. Funk knows the subject, but her teaching style is not for everyone. She uses the Flipped Classroom method for her algorithms class and you have to learn all the content yourself and come to class and do group worksheets. She's also a slow grader and it's hard to reach out to her outside of class. Get ready to work, it's definitely a hard class.
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Not a fan of the class personally, but she is a genuine person who wants her students to learn. A lot of busy work but pretty lenient grading. Lectures can get pretty boring at times but again that's more due to the course structure. Would recommend her for this class, but not other ones
too much busy work.

Dr. Funk seems like a nice lady, but the amount of busy work she assigns in this class is comical. You need to read the textbook in order to pass the exams and write "defense summaries", which is a fancy way of summarizing the textbook and also making test questions. Reaching her via email is near impossible. The course isn't too hard to pass.

Well um uh, well she isn't really the uh, um but the thing is uh..

See how the sentence made absolutely no sense, this is precisely how she lectures. It's impossible to tag along and understand what she is saying with all of the tangents and rambling that occur. On top of that, it is impossible to get a hold of her over email or other online ways,

Honestly, I don't think she understands the materials well enough herself. She's often makes mistakes and then corrects herself, making lectures VERY confusing. Her tests are usually pretty fair though (given how she teaches). I recommend reading the textbook if you can; that's how I survived each exam. Attendance is like 9% of the grade I believe.

She's a nice person but doesn't teach too well. I've heard that our content is very different from what other classes are learning. The HW and tests cover stuff she doesn't always teach (these are made by the TA though). She does try her best to teach. But I prob wouldn't take her, it's a hard class the way she teaches it.

She had a rough semester with a loss of a family member and breaking both legs not even a month later. she still seemed to put the students interests first. The class was not bad just a lot of busy work and the exams are from questions made by students as homework that she selects so they could be about anything. Still could have been much worse

flipped classroom but structured in an awful way. randomly assigned groups for worksheets every week. takes forever to grade things. literally on the first day of class said to not email her because she wont respond.

Flipped classroom, daily group wksts, difficult exams, harsh grader, slow grades, stubborn, too many assignments. Hard class where I barely learned anything. Don't take Funk until she restructures her course for algorithms.

The flipped classrooms, group worksheets, and two-part exams were challenging and unclear. Grades are unorganized and she takes a long time to grade. Make sure you have a strong retention of discrete math and theory concepts. The class is a lot of self learning and you'll spend many hours outside of class.

Awful Experience, The questions on the tests and especially the HW are on content she doesn't cover in class. She only reads off the McGraw Hill slides and attendance is mandatory. I went to several tutors that have aced the class in other sections, and they were just as confused on the HW content. Just wait for a better professor next semester.

I feel she gets a lot of hate for the format of the class but it seems shes required by UGA to teach it this way. She is extremely nice and understanding so its really unfortunate that the class is the way that it is. She is very caring and I feel like I would have enjoyed the class much more if it was not formatted to have so much busy work.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Caring

reviews.comment: mostly busy work the lectures are unnecessary most of the time. you would be hard pressed to find lecture material on the exam since she asks students to write the questions for it as part of their homework. If you ever have the feeling you forgot an assignment in this class, you did because something new felt like it was due every day
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: Shelby Funk uses a flipped classroom method where she likes having students do worksheets in groups while she releases some videos every week. A lot of prior knowledge is needed in this course like discrete math and data structures where very specific information is needed. You also have to worry about having good group members as well

I can tell that Professor Funk is very passionate and knowledgeable on the subject, but she makes the in class worksheets extremely difficult. They always have questions that are beyond the scope of what she teaches, which is taught outside of class time since it is a flipped classroom. The random group assignments for the worksheets suck, too.

She expects you to use a ridiculous format on assignments including a title page with who the document is prepared for (her ofc) and what city & state you are working in (Athens duh) and if this incorrect she will doc 10 points. She feeds her political opinions in the class the lectures are boring and she is always late but it is a required course
Very caring and wants to foster a collaborative setting most of the time
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Professor Funk's class involves a lot of group work and will probably force you to put a lot of time in outside of class. However, her exams are not extraordinarily difficult if you study using the worksheets and the homework. She is a very kind and caring professor and is always happy to help. She puts in great effort to prepare you.

honestly shes very nice and always willing to help/explain concepts, the biggest pain point of the class was that you have to self learn all the content from textbook/lecture videos and every assignment is also done in groups. exams weren't too bad, they were mostly the same difficulty as the classwork so if you go to class then its all good

The worst CS class I've taken at UGA. Entire course is completely busy work. Exam questions are about dumb specific things. "Defense summaries" are you making test questions for the professor and rewriting what was in the text books in your own words. Breakout sessions take 3 hours of your day to speak one time. Complete waste of everybody's time.

Aside from the fact that this class is utterly useless (and overly and overtly political at times), Dr. Funk is an ok professor. Test questions are very specific and even unrelated to Ethics most of the time. Lectures are boring, but she does elaborate on certain topics and tries at times to add some interest. Don't even bother trying to email her.

Dr. Funk explains topics well in class. This class is hard, but she is a fair exam grader. Unfortunately, it's hard to learn the material from her online lecture videos, and you have 40 group worksheets, which is too many, since the TA only had time to grade half our homeworks. But you'll be fine if you make CLRS your Bible and worship it daily.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Group projects--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Dr. Funk is a nice professor, but she is not accessible outside of class. It is very hard to get an email response from her. The structure of the course is the same, but there are a lot of assignments that eventually become busy work. Attendance is mandatory and a part of your grade. If you keep track of deadlines, the course isn't hard.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Professors like Dr. Funk are why nobody takes the CS department at UGA seriously. She does not teach. She transposes the text of the textbook onto some slides and reads. There is mandatory attendance since she probably knows that no rational person would actually waste their time with coming to this class when the textbook teaches better than her.
Utterly Terrible. So awful.

Pretty easy A. Lectures could sometimes be boring, and it is difficult to get ahold of her by email. Otherwise, this class is great. You just have to do a weekly video that is a few min about an ethical issue and upload it on tophat. So there is some work involved but as long as you do all the work you will get an A

Dr. Funk was engaging in lectures and encouraged active participation. However, there were frequent technical issues and errors on due dates that caused a lot of undue stress. It was also nearly impossible to get an email response from her in a timely manner. That being said, she resolved most of these issues and is an incredibly generous person.

I liked Dr. Funk's lectures, I think she did a really good job encouraging participation and understanding people's points. The material was pretty interesting. The class format is repetitive, so it's always clear what you need to do, even if it can feel like a chore at times. Easy A as long as you keep up with the work.

Not even as bad as everyone claims her to be, but the class she teaches is an absolute slog of busywork, and you don't end up learning anything meaningful. She's a nice professor, but I found it was hard to get a response from her through email.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Lots of homework--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Shelby Funk is literally so horrible. I cant put into words how much I absolutely despised this class and the way it was run. I haven't been given this much unless work in a classroom since I was in kindergarten and was picking my nose and drawing on the wall to pass the time, it was 100% busywork. She also doesn't care about the students at all.

Do not take Funk! Unreachable outside of class and only assigns 2 homework (so each is about 11% of your grade). Class is full of busy work but your midterm and final are about 50% of your total grade. Only take if you are okay with not knowing your grades for most of the semester.
reviews.ratingTags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Dr. Funk has been ignoring several emails from me about 2 assignments I couldnt finish due to a technical issue with TopHat, which has brought the grade of two of my final assignments down from a 90-100 to 35s for each of them. She doesnt really seem to care. If you take 3030, you wont learn anything, but attendance is still somehow mandatory.

Extremely rude in correspondence, unhelpful, uninterested in teaching or students needs. Fails to maintain even the minimum standards of professionalism within a teaching context. Stunningly disorganized. In the interest of fairness, she is reputably a very kind woman outside of class
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: The semester ends in 4 days and I have no idea what I have in the class. Extremely slow grader and takes a while to respond to student emails. Grades are based on group worksheets which aren't that bad, homework which is based on nothing from the lectures (which don't go deep at all), and quizzes and tests which are around the same difficulty as HW

Don't take online CSCI2670 with her. You get online group worksheets every Mon-Thurs, mostly surface level explanations of concepts in lectures (especially as you approach more difficult material), slow as hell grading, homework on stuff you weren't taught, etc. Interesting material, presented in a demotivating, messy, and unengaging manner.
reviews.ratingTags: Lecture heavy--Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects

reviews.comment: Stay 6 feet away from her, i personally would find any other professor i swear on my life dont take her
The literal worst professor I have ever had in my entire academic career. Not even close. Took a big toll on my mental health and anxiety.

Professor Funk was definitely not the best. Communication was poor and grading was very slow. However, this class wasnt that hard and mainly just a lot busywork and assignments. That being said, in a more difficult class I would make an effort to avoid taking it with her.
Absolutely terrible class and the professor was no help. A lot of homework assignments and quiz. Lecture examples are no help in solving the assignments. Avoid this professor at all costs!

You may be wondering why "hilarious". It's because this class is hilariously terrible. Worst experience of my life. Teacher has no communication with students, easy lectures but HARDEST worksheets ever, 1-2 example problems. Reuses lecture videos from 3 years ago. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS with her!
reviews.ratingTags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Hilarious--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Class is easy, but Funk was probably one of the worst professors i've had at Uga. It took almost two months to grade our midterms she's very inconsistent.

The class was fairly easy - mostly just lots of busywork. Funk is very inconsistent though. She would have wrong due dates and not check Piazza for a week at a time and this only got worse as the semester went on. The fact that she didn't seem to try at all to improve and placed the burden on us when she messed up was extremely irritating.

She seems like a nice lady, but thats about the end of the positives. She's very absent minded in terms of classwork. She teaches in videos she posts, and in class only goes over previous worksheets. This leaves you on your own if you don't understand the material after the video, and the way she words all her questions leave you confused.
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Group projects

reviews.comment: If you're reading reviews and deciding on whether to take Funk for 2670, don't do it! After every class period there is a worksheet due that night. Lectures cover easy examples while the problems on the worksheet are EXTREMELY hard. Promised to post midterm test grades before drop/add and posted them TWO WEEKS AFTER. Class average was mid 50's.

It's not hard to get a good grade just a TON of busywork, doing the same thing every single week. Waste of my time, learned nothing. No communication, haven't seen her since syllabus day, only go to class for breakouts where we talk about nothing for 30 minutes until the TA pops in and we say random stuff for 2 minutes until he leaves.

There are not enough characters available on why you should not take this professor. One of the worst I've ever taken. She does not respond to students and her lecture examples are much easier than the actual assignments. She asked the class for feedback on her teaching after the class got a 57% average on her midterm, but did not change anything.
reviews.ratingTags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Group projects--So many papers

reviews.comment: One of the worst professors at UGA. Lack of communication. Extremely unorganized and confused for majority of the class. Constantly giving tedious busy work. Lectures are 100x times easier than the HW and Test making them very unreliable. Beyond slow at grading, waits for drop date deadline to pass. Will put you down if given the chance. Avoid!!!!

Not the best for this class, and not the best professor overall. She thinks giving an assignment every day is somehow learning, but it just feels like a chore. Definitely take this class with Hollingsworth, if you can, and go out of your way to learn the material from YouTube since the material for this class is actually really cool.

If you wanna feel like you're in high school again then take this class. Just a ton of busy work and you don't really learn anything. You're pretty much doing the same thing over and over again throughout the semester, and it is just mind numbing tedious work. It's not hard to get a good grade, but no communication from the professor.
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: The amount of busywork in this class was insane. Material was good at first, but it got to the point where it was just braindead busywork. The teacher never communicated or gave us feedback and made it clear our class wasn't important to her. Took her days to get back to us on Piazza about important issues. She was super unprofessional overall.

Never rated a professor before but just had to for her cause she was so bad. No communication, she barely gave us any information before our final exam. Really disorganized, she still had the same grade criteria from the summer classes so all the grades on elc were jumbled up. The most braindead class you can ever take.

Easily the most disorganized class that I've ever taken at UGA. Dr. Funk doesn't respect your time. Teacher-in-name-only. A ridiculous, stuffy amount of work every week and it hardly feels meaningful. Actually never graded our weekly coursework, or at least there's no indication of it at the end of the semester. We were afterthoughts to her.
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Participation matters--So many papers

reviews.comment: Very disorg'd class; weekly busy work became a chore as class went on. Breakout articles got progressively less interesting & thought provoking. More than once, modules would release late. Waittime for grades: weeks. Some due dates had relaxed enforcement, but not quizzes & defense summaries. Fast answers to Qs except during Thanksgiving break.
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Accessible outside class--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: CSCI 3030 was the most disorganized class I have taken. I felt like she did not care about this class at all because most tophat assignments are posted late, her elc dates are outdated from LAST semester, refuses to fix her elc grading book (midterm was under final). Be prepared for tedious work, repetitive assignments and dry breakout sessions.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Participation matters--So many papers

reviews.comment: Lots of busywork and very disorganized. Slow at grading, so many of us didn't know our grade until the day of the drop deadline. Little feedback on tests and quizzes. Vague on participation requirements for breakout sessions so we never knew if we got full participation.
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--So many papers

reviews.comment: This semester, all 3030 students used the materials from Dr. Cotterell (the other ethics teacher) because she was previously very disorganized, so it was clear what was expected of us. The lectures are based on the textbook, but she makes it a little more interesting. As long as you do the readings, you should easily pass.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Video lectures outside of class, daily worksheets in groups, some HW, and little lecturing. She explains the concepts you need to know but not very well as she expects you to get very abstract thinking. Note taking and studying were essential for me to comprehend. She is a great person and is willing to help you learn, but you need to try too.
reviews.ratingTags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: We got videos to watch at home and then had worksheets in groups every day of class. Worksheets are graded and are 8% of the grade and attendance is another 12%. We got 4 homework assignments consisting of 4 problems from the book each for 15%. The other 65% come from tests that're decently hard. If you put in any effort you'll do fine.
reviews.ratingTags: Gives good feedback--Participation matters--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: She is horrible lecturer. Attendance is mandatory. ashe doesnt care about her students. She added .03 to curve grade like that does anything. Doesnt round your grade. if you get a 89.96 u will get B+ instead of A-. Overall horrible teacher.
reviews.ratingTags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: A great professor if you don't need to learn anything. I would highly recommend for csci3030, but would not recommend for any actually important classes. Lectures are boring and slides have no words on them. Attendance is mandatory. Textbook is not necessary if you pay attention in class, but is needed for test studying if you don't (like me).

2essays allowed to rewrite them for a better grade 2discussion posts 1project and breakout sessions She is all over place with the assignments that were supposed to be due We were supposed to have 3essays,4discussion posts and she uses umm a lot and doesnt give you a clear explanation on whats on the exam overall she was nice sweet and easy to pass
Dr. Funk is a tough grader and never curve the grade for master student. Her test is very hard.

Extremely tough grader, I've been getting sub 50% grades on papers that were not bad. I normally excel at essay writing and have almost never gotten less than an A on a paper. For a class that most people would expect to be an easy A this is way too hard.

Unorganized and confusing. Don't ask questions because she will make it even more confusing. Topics are not terribly difficult, but somehow she makes it harder than it needs to be. She is also super slow to get tests back. *RECOMMENDATION* She teaches the engineering version of the class- just take the comp-sci version instead to avoid her.

Dr. Funk is genuinely caring, but super forgetful and scattered. She oftentimes took weeks to grade simple things, and her TAs grade overly harsh for simply things like discussion grades & never said why points off. Attendance is 4% of your grade, which is just annoying and kinda pointless. Teaches directly from the book. Wouldn't recommend

Waits nearly a month to hand back exams, does not have a well organized and structured lecture, and does not talk with students when concerns are asked online. She seemed very ill-prepared for each lecture. This definitely showed when she had to search YouTube for a sorting algorithm she tried 10 minutes to figure out but still could not.

Her lectures aren't done in class. it's 45 minutes of lecture videos online due at the beginning of the week. The rest of the time is group work. Not very helpful if you don't understand. Avoid at all cost.

Dr. Funk is really well organized. She puts a lot of effort in making her slides and examples and is thoroughly prepared most of the time. The class content itself is pretty difficult so be ready to work hard. This class assumes you are coming in with a very solid discrete math background. If you don't I would suggest taking an online course first.
Prof Funk is one of the best teacher I ever had. She does a great job making  complex material easy to understand.  Take her.

Terrible teacher and a bad grader. The final grade always has an element of surprise. If you flatter her, it curves in your favor, else you are gone. Never ever take a class under this lady. Avoid as far as possible.
Horrible teacher, unorganized. Writes tests at the last minute. If you have no prior knowledge to discrete math do not take her. Actually just to be safe, don't take her at all!

Her actions speak for themselves. Our three tests were returned the last few days of the semester. I actually never got back my first because she finished grading it after the last day of class. The final was 80% new material despite telling us verbatim "It's cumulative. You have to know everything" when asked if the new material was more important
Horrible teacher and a bad grader. Highly Judgmental and is extremely biased towards lady students!

Dr. Funk is a very good teacher. She knows what she's doing, and does her best to convey the concepts to the class with plenty of examples. Only thing I disliked about the course is that homework is graded fairly strictly (for accuracy) and counts a good chunk of your grade (20-30%). If you must take the course I highly recommend Dr. Funk!

Dr. Funk might seem kind of scatterbrained at times, and you'll definitely have to work to keep up, but she knows her stuff. You'll be better prepared for other CS courses after Discrete Math with her. Don't take this class if you're obsessed with getting an A though; right now nobody in my class of 20+ has one.

The course is light, but it's definitely a class where it's what you make it out to be. His lectures are monotone and boring, and he has a heavy accent. I will say I learned a lot from his homework, however. They helped you understand the material, but the overall structure of the class was quite disappointing for a class I was looking forward to.
Dr. Liang is not a great professor. His lectures are extremely boring and his accent is difficult to understand. He's an extremely hard grader. Regardless of that, the class is very easy.

I desperately wanted to give him above 2 stars, but he has a very difficult to understand accent. VERY difficult. He's knowledgeable about the stuff I guess, but his lectures are hard to focus on or understand. IF YOU HAVE GOOD CODING EXPERIENCE THIS CLASS IS A BREEZE! If you have none, SELF TEACH, the lectures won't help.

Tends to ramble and often emphasizes the “weed out” nature of the class, which isn't encouraging for beginners without prior background. Later lectures didn't prepare well for the time‑consuming labs, and no extra practice was provided outside of notes/quizzes as a way to study leading to me to rely on other resources.

Mr. Liang really seemed to care for the students and occasionally made jokes which not enough people laughed to. Not the best at teaching the course material but if you end up with him its not the end of the world.

This class is designed to be difficult but Dr. Liang somehow makes it worse.  His lectures have no correlation with the ongoing projects and labs and he rambles a lot. I didn't learn anything useful in this class. Don't bother asking for extra credit b/c he doesn't give any.

Be prepared for long lectures that could have been summarized in 15 minutes. Lots of quizzes, labs, and projects, but very doable and mostly fun. Two exams that accounted for 50% of the final grade. The exams can be tough for beginners and can drop final grades very easily. The textbook helped a lot.

Dr Liang is very caring, however he tends to ramble a lot. This paired with his accent makes it very difficult to actually absorb anything in class when it's surrounded by 50+ tangents. Grading for the class tends to be quite anal, as labs are graded by software so any slight spelling or capitalization difference leads to missed points.

This professor is very knowledgeable, however, I felt like sometimes going to class was a waste of time due to how much he rambles. Graded by few things, Project, quizzes, and a midterm and final exam are the only 2 tests.

I found this class to be a complete joke and waste of time. The material could be summed up in a 10 minute video. The final project was the only thing that mattered and odds are you will be placed in a group that has no idea what they are doing. I did 95% of the work because these kids didnt know how to use html or how to show up to meetings.

I took Dr. Liang for 1301 and 4050 and had a positive experience both times. Although the first day of class he really does sound like a stickler for participation, he's extremely understanding about missing in-class assignments if you email him. His exams were straightforward. He's definitely not the organized, but he makes up by being lenient.
Class isn't too hard, but he does not know how to teach. He often takes the entire class time to teach one line of code. Super picky grading as well.
The worst professor at UGA. He does not reply to emails and his teaching style is poor. Do not take him.
Dr. Liang is a decent lecturer, and he definitely knows what he is talking about, but he rambles a lot. He also really enjoys when people struggle/fail in his class, so you need to work hard.

Dr. Liang is an okay lecturer, yet there's a concerning lack of transparency in his grading policies. The day of the final exam he informed us that exam grades wouldn't be disclosed; rather, he'd determine our final grades himself. This seems unjust when the exam weighs in a quarter of the overall grade.

Was apprehensive since he's never taught Software Engineering before, but overall I've had a very positive experience. Although he's not the most organized and his accent is fairly heavy, he's very kind and understanding if you just reach out to him. The midterm exam was straightforward I feel as though he's a pretty lenient grader.
One of the better CS profs I've had at UGA. Has a fairly heavy accent but is very kind and understanding if you just reach out and talk to him.

I had him my first semester here and the class was difficult for me having not done any coding before. Not the best lectures in my opinion but an A is very doable as long as you keep up with the notes and learn from the assignments. Two exams that make up 50% of the grade, lots of labs and projects, plus quizzes.

In my opinion, he is not as bad most reviews say, though he will be difficult if you're new to CS (or if you have skill issues). The class is very standardized so there are clear requirements from students. Attendance was not mandatory, and the final was easy imo.

Worst instructor I have ever had in my entire 19-year life. Taking away 30% of my lab grades for misspelling a single word. When I asked him any question, he was just so defensive and never tried to actually address the problem. He was so proud of his class being rigorous and having a high failing rate. Always try to trick students. Just don't.

This class is hard if you do not have previous knowledge in Java or other coding languages. I already took this class in high school and retook it and it was still difficult for me. He is hard to understand sometimes in lecture because he talks very fast. Labs are unusually hard for a beginner class.

He has very helpful explanations in class and develops a good understanding of the nuances of the course. I believe that he truly cares about the students, and the difficulty that most people have is with the course more than the instructor. CSCI 1301 is just a hard course. Be careful, labs and quizzes will trip you up and can be low grades easily.
reviews.ratingTags: Clear grading criteria--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Liang is a half-decent professor who catches a lot of slack due to how difficult the course he teaches is. The class is all lecture but having some form of Java knowledge is critical to do well in the class. Dr. Liang is a good professor, but prepare yourself for a lot of work and coding that is consistent in all CS classes.
reviews.ratingTags: Lecture heavy--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: In my opinion, he's definitely not a terrible teacher, nor is he an excellent teacher. If you actually pay attention in his class and attend most of his lectures, you should be good. Although there were some concepts that he didn't explain the absolute best, you eventually get it after a while because he makes sure to show you examples in class.

I was discouraged to take this professor at first, but I really enjoyed him this past semester. In order to succeed in this class, you need to pay attention to his lectures and ask any questions (even with labs and projects). He is truly a nice guy and doesn't purposefully try to harm your grade. Computer Science is hard, don't blame the professor.

Dr. Liang can be scattered and hard to understand at times, but he isn't a bad professor. The only bad thing about this course is the grading (especially for the lab) is quite harsh. For example, I forgot to capitalize one word and got a 76%. The lab section is handled entirely by TAs though so can't even really blame him for it.

Do not take 1301 with this dude under any circumstances. 50% of grade is based on two tests. He does not provide any study materials for these, even the final. His announcements to the class, and email responses, are often rude and condescending. Lectures are nonsensical, and powerpoints are hardly insightful, despite these being all he provides.

He isn't bad. Taken a few years ago, he has a very strong accent, and many of his tests were based on information from class. All you have to do is follow along in class, take notes, and really write down code examples. Extra clarification was needed infrequently, even with no prior Java experience. It really just takes some effort.
reviews.ratingTags: Participation matters--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: For CSCI 1301, this is the worst lecturer you will ever have. Please, if you can avoid him at all costs, do so. He really takes pleasure in giving you poor grades, and his accent is very evident. This is not an introduction, so come with some Java knowledge. Be prepared for this class.

Idk why he gets so much hate; he seems very sweet and caring. Heavy accent and yes boring lectures but he does what he needs to. All assessments are nicely spaced with only 2 tests. I do only recommend this course for ppl who have experience in CS. I only did bad bc I suck at CS, not bc he is a bad professor. Maybe not the best, but still great !

Whether you have CS experience or not, DONT take this teacher. He is awful. Doesn't care for the students whatsoever. Was the only teacher to offer zero extra credit/help. His lectures were a complete waste of time. His sole purpose was to get us to fail. Tutoring is vital to passing this class. Use the java book to understand concepts. Good luck!
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS. His lectures are super hard to follow, he just reads over a couple slides and does a few examples. He does not care about his students' grades or learning at all. The midterm and final is 25% of our grade and there is NO study session or study guide. unorganized, no extra credit, & does not provide right answers for quizzes.

This class was very useless. The lectures were unorganized, impossible to follow and there was never any explanations given, only examples done on the projector quickly. The entire class was graded on a few confusing quizzes and poorly written tests.

dont even try it two tests make up 50% of ur grade. labs and projects are not too bad but grading is extremlly harsh, if the output requires ONE capital letter and u have a lower case its an immediate 10 points off. lectures are extremly unorganized and u learn nothing. if u taking it first semester, learn java over the summer. if 2nd sem good luck

Prof Liang assigns quizzes that are DELIBERATELY written to confuse you, not to test your knowledge. If you ask a question that is not specifically within a class example he cannot explain it or answer it. I wouldn't recommend my worst enemy to sign up for this professor's class. Extremely pedantic, unhelpful, and rude responding to emails. 0/5....
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: He basically admitted he enjoys giving out bad grades to his students.. He would show up with bed head every single class, read off the slides, and code some basic examples that weren't useful. He is literally unable to explain concepts such that beginners would understand it. He can't answer questions and blames the student for not understanding.
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Professor Liang is extremely intelligent and he wants you to know that. Despite this, he is unable to explain concepts in a way that beginners would understand. The grading is excessively harsh and one bad grade will basically fail you for the semester. He doesn't provide a good environment to learn in and I wouldn't recommend anyone take his class
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Liang seems like a super nice guy, his lectures were just not helpful. He stands at the front and codes with little to no explanation. The class itself would not be bad if you're experienced in cs. I had to teach myself all the material with little to no prof/ TA help. Two paper exams that make your grade. Not something I would do again.

I withdrew from his class, he gives us a talk at the beginning about who should drop. This is a top 10 difficulty undergrad class at UGA even though it's an intro. DO NOT TAKE IT unless you already know how to code in java or you're very good at learning java on your own.

this class is a pain. if you dont HAVE to take it, dont. the lectures don't relate to the labs and projects and nothing relates to the exam material. the labs and projects take up so much time and do almost nothing for your final grade, it all comes down to the midterm and final. for an "intro" class it's impossible to keep up.

This course gave me PTSD. I have no words for how awful this was. I have gone through the UGA chem department and I would rather take them any day than this course. The lectures are completely unhelpful as in he did not give lectures that helped with the assignments. Like he didnt teach anything. I hope you have tech friends who know how to code.

The problem with this class is that although you may have a really high project and lab average, that only makes up 30% of your final grade. The midterm and final are 50% and are very difficult. He is never available for questions, which leaves you to ask the TAs who want to be there even less than you do.
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Get ready to read--Test heavy

reviews.comment: Almost impossible to get an A (94). TA's grade ruthlessly, and will take off points even when not specified in grading. Liang is condescending, and in many cases gives incorrect information. Unclear grading, mediocre lectures, and overall waste of time. He once told me he LIKES giving Bs and Cs because "scars drive people forward." Regret taking :(

The labs are graded ruthlessly by the TAs, and the projects later in the semester take many days to complete. There are two tests that are 50% of the grade, which are hard and cover material not on the ppt. There is no extra credit, so it's nearly impossible to get an A (which is a 94) and your grade will suffer if you mess up on one assignment.

Although he posted all the PPTs, quizzes and the final were still so detail oriented, with some information not covered in the PPTs. He assigned labs every week, and sometimes had a quiz and project also due the same week. The class was still hard even with some coding languages, and he offered no help with the labs. TA's were ruthless with labs.
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Clear grading criteria--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: Hard class if you don't have coding experience. Biweekly quizzes, labs weekly, a project biweekly too. Only 2 tests, which are 50% of grade, and are hard. Lots of work outside of class. Prof isn't much help, if any. He's hard to understand and very rude (esp via email). Doesn't give excuses for anything, so turn things in perfectly, and on time.

The worst prof at UGA. Doesn't care abt students at all. Any reason my assignments were late (not often) were not taken into account, even if proof was given. Lectures were not that useful, talks really fast to understand. Ppts are posted on eLC, only good part. Isn't accessible outside class for help. Responds to emails fast, but very rude.

This class is unnecessarily hard. The TA's grade really harsh and as someone with no coding experience coming into this class, I've never felt so underprepared for the final exam even after studying a lot. The quizzes and tests don't check to see if you know the concept or not. They deliberately use wordplay to trip you up.
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: eh, lectures can be hard to follow, have coding experience before you come to this class because you will be lost

Dr. Liang is knowledgeable about the material, but he is not the best at teaching it. The course is not spread out properly. There are weekly labs and sometimes there are projects and quizzes at the same time. The TAs grade hard, so make sure you thoroughly check your work before submitting it. Definitely requires more work outside of class.

Class isn't too bad if you don't skip but if you do he has PowerPoints that he posts online after the lecture. He is very passionate about what he teaches. Nice in person but the rudest ever via email. If you email him ANYTHING it will be the most passive aggressive response imaginable. TA's are picky when grading. It's an okay class though.

Nice guy but lectures are extremely boring and can be hard to understand. Labs are difficult but the TA will be your best friend if you ask a lot of questions. Grading TAs take points for anything so pay attention to detail. The two tests are confusing for no reason and are 50% of grade. Definitely a difficult class if you have no coding experience
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Not the best. He knows what he's doing, but he's not the best at teaching. You're better off coming in with some experience in code; otherwise, you might not do well. The class itself is not tough if you have experience, but the tests are very hard. Never needed/read the textbook. Never got our grades for the final either because we all did so bad.

Professor Liang is a really nice guy, but unfortunately not a very good professor. He never posted a class schedule all year, our quiz grades were never accessible, and his teaching style was not very effective. If you don't have any previous coding experience then this class will be quite difficult.

Honestly, you need some previous Java experience to do well in this class. I took APCSA in high school so I didn't struggle as much as others. Liang is nice, but his way of teaching is inefficient and boring. Everyone has their laptops open during class, so who knows if people are actually paying attention or playing 2048 for 75 minutes.
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Liang is DEFINITELY a better option than Lamarca for 1301 students. His lectures were hard to follow at first, but paying attention in class is super important because he codes examples and strategies we would use for labs. I came in with no coding experience at all before this class and I was able to get an A, but it required studying a lot.
reviews.ratingTags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Liang is not accommodating at all. If you miss a test he will not let you make it up. Lectures are disorganized and tests are unnecessarily confusing. If you are not experienced in coding this class will be extremely hard. Undergrad TAs grade all the labs and they grade ruthlessly, take off points for the smallest things. Impossible to make an A.
reviews.ratingTags: Graded by few things--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I entered this class with some prior Java experience so I wasn't too dependent on his lectures. Thank god, cause they were NOT good. His accent is fine but his English is under par and lectures are disorganized. Labs are graded by undergrad TAs who grade ruthlessly. The two tests, which are a combined 50% of the grade, are unnecessarily confusing.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Test heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: He's a decent professor. Yes, he can be a bit hard to understand at times, but he genuinely cares about his students and listens to their feedback. He's very passionate about his teaching and gives an ample amount of time to prepare for quizzes. He is significantly better than Lamarca, so if you had to choose, take Liang.

If you coded before, this class will probably be a breeze. If not, It will probably be a struggle. He's got a pretty thick accent but is still understandable, his lectures are very boring, the TA's on the labs can be very hit or miss. Some can take off points everywhere while others will just give 100s.

Of the professors at UGA in the CS Department if you are choosing a professor I would definitely go with Liang. He is an extremely fair teacher and much better than Lamarac. I came into 1301 with some coding knowledge with made the class much easier. If you are struggling with topics the textbook provided will be a lot of help.

These bad reviews aren't a reflection of Yiheng, he is a good professor and is really passionate about comp sci. The bad reviews reflect UGA's computer science department-specifically intro. If you have little to know coding, this will be a challenge for you. Fast paced, TA's grade harshly. But he is a really great guy and put effort into his class

Honestly if you've got coding experience this class is light work. For those who have none, this is probably gonna be a challenge. I would suggest reading the textbook and going to tutoring if you are struggling. The 95 to get the 4.0 is kinda ridiculous but I really enjoy going to class and it is one of the few classes I'm not incredibly bored in.
reviews.ratingTags: Gives good feedback--Skip class? You won't pass.--Test heavy

reviews.comment: UGA doesnt have many good 1301 professors and that's a fact. Liang is not amazing but he does try his best. His accent and stuttering make it a bit hard for him to understand but his lectures are pretty solid. Labs and projects are graded by TAs and theyre very harsh on it but you can go to Liang to ask for some points back if it wasnt fair grading

Grade is made up of 6 doable quizzes, one midterm, one final, labs/projects, and participation points. other lecturer for 1301 gave 13 hard quizzes and no participation points to help final grade. Followed a flipped classroom model and showed many examples rather than just reading off the powerpoint. notes are posted on ELC.

Makes me sad to see other people's reviews without them factoring in how good this professor is compared to other CSCI teachers at UGA. The accent is more than fine. Wish he would control the classroom better, but solid lecturer.

Liang tends to ramble a lot in his lectures, but he's otherwise a great professor. He cares about his students and is very willing to help your grade or explain something to you one-on-one if you don't understand - all you have to do is ask! 1301 is difficult if you have never coded before, so ask as many questions as you can.

His lectures may be slightly slow and he will stick to a topic for most of the class, but he was a good professor. The class itself wasn't hard and there was not a lot of homework, the only homework we got was a lab a week and maybe one project. The quizzes were mentioned in class before and grading was done by TAs, some graded harder than others.

This guy honestly has no idea what he is doing. He walks into the class and starts lecturing while the rest of the class is talking. He doesn't know how to teach a class at all. The grade needed for an A is a 95 which is nearly impossible since you only have 1 midterm and a final. The labs toward the end of the semester take 10+ hours and are hard.
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Like I said, respected. I don't believe there is a professor who demands more respect than this man. Reading these reviews I can see the other students truly do have an immense amount of respect for him. He is quite possibly the most imperious man I have ever met. Take this class if you'd like to learn a thing or two about respect.

I think that he genuinely tries his best at teaching. Coding is very difficult and it is a difficult class, so going to the lectures definitely helps. A lot of students will sit in lecture and play games or do work for other courses... but if you take notes and copy the code he writes, it is very helpful towards our lab assignments and projects.

I was so excited to start Comp sci- didnt have a lot of experience which should be fine in an intro class BUT this class failed me. I still feel so lost and I changed my major. TAs take off so many points for little mistakes. 2 tests, takes forever to grade things. Dont take this if you dont have a lot of experience coding.
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Liang is a good professor if you have previous coding experience. If you don't, you're going to struggle a ton and will need help. I learned a lot from tutoring and online coding courses. Plus, if you tend to have a hard time understanding people with a thick accent, you will not learn anything in his lecture. Oh, and the TAs grade labs ruthlessly.

If you have no previous experience coding then you will struggle in this class. I feel as if I was not taught how to code properly and the course moves very fast. Try to get some experience from a coding bootcamp and maybe you will do good. I switched my major after this class.

If your first language is english you will not understand what this man says. He has a very heavy accent. If you already have knowledge of java this class is very easy compared to Lamarca. If you don't, good luck if you take this man.

I hope I will never see this person in my life again. His syllabus/explanations regarding any graded items are vague and unclear. He often notifies about the quiz at the last minute and acts like we only take his class. It usually takes 2 weeks or more for us to grade our 4 question quizzes and even longer for lab assignments. He can't teach either

Knows a lot about computer science, only graded on tests and final project. Goes very quickly and can be hard to understand. Tests were unnecessarily hard compared to the class. Did not feel like the lecture material prepared me for the exams. Very nice teacher but would not take again.

Liang is a good professor and seems super passionate about CS. Lectures are super boring and you honestly don't need to pay attention if you just study the PPTs or textbook. Labs start easy but get hard as you go and the TA graders are hella strict. If you make a tiny mistake in your code you get a ton of points off so be careful with that.

Boring lectures, but she's very unintentionally funny, so it makes it bearable. Disorganized and makes mistakes on assignments and lectures, but won't punish you for her mistakes. Exams were pretty fair, and homework could be a little tedious, but not that bad. If you ask her about Attack on Titan, she will like you.

She really tries to make her lectures fun, but constantly doubts herself and changes slides mid-lecture, making it really confusing. If she were to read her slides before lecture, this could have been avoided.  There are always errors on her assignments, and she is so disorganized. Tests are tough, and she usually curves them by dropping questions
You'll be fine passing her class. I sympathize with the comments here, but I don't really care too much about the quality of her content, bc all I did was keep everything organized and I passed.
Don't bother going to class, she just reads off the slides. Just study the slides on the schedule and practice with the example code skeletons she provides.

I've never experienced a professor make so many mistakes in lecture, quizzes, and tests. Couldn't really teach well, but if you study, her quizzes/tests are pretty easy. You're fine grade wise if you take her, but you really learn NOTHING, which is bad for a class as important as DS. Also, she loves disco and geocaching, so mention those lol.

She is extremely disorganized. Lectures feel pretty pointless and a lot needs to be learned on your own. Tests are just the quizzes but harder, there are homework redos, and some extra credit. You should do fine in terms of grades but as for actually learning, i felt like i learned very little.

Dr. Maria may be a nice person overall, but as a professor she truly is one of if not the worst I have ever experienced at UGA. Her weekly quizzes always have mistakes in them that require you to rework through the quiz. Her lectures are never organized, and her grading is awful as she always marks off for tasks even though they are shown complete
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Disorganized but doesn't penalize you for her own mistakes. Lectures don't help with assignments, so they're time-consuming to figure them out yourself and mess around. Workload is heavy, and some projects can be difficult. She's friendly and funny, so you can't really hate her. Very understanding with somewhat lenient deadlines.

The workload is equivalent to like 3 classes of work. Oftentimes you're working with really complicated concepts, linear algebra, matrix transformations. The class is really fun you'll learn A LOT, also the final projects are really cool to see at the end of the semester. There's 2 presentations, and a quiz every wednesday. If you lock in, u get A.
reviews.ratingTags: Participation matters--Lots of homework--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment:
Good: great willingness to help students

Bad: tendency to ramble, long lectures sometimes unrelated to projects/homework, unfair balance of workload that is considerably heavier towards the end of the term, inaccessible via email.

She's the funniest professor I've ever had—understanding, caring, and lenient with late work, though the workload is demanding. I learned so much and had a lot of fun. Assignments were given ample time (over a week), and she's fair with deadlines. If we turned in something late due to submission boxes opening late, she never penalized us.

Yes, prof is quite disorganized and it can be frustrating, but she would never give a low grade based on her own mistake. She's flexible and gives extensions/late days. As of this semester, this class does not have any exams, just HW/projects, attendance quizzes with predictable dates. She handwaves through lectures so if you hate that, stay away.
reviews.ratingTags: Participation matters--EXTRA CREDIT--Beware of pop quizzes

reviews.comment: By far the worst professor I've ever had. Although the prof is nice, she runs the class in such a disorganized way that it is detrimental. Assignments get posted on the due date, and the instructions usually are unclear and misspelled. The syllabus is vague and the grading rubrics make no sense.
Easily the worst professor I have ever had. Has no domain knowledge of the class subjects, and makes tons of mistakes on homework and projects.

this class was a pain in the behind. but anything worth learning is. im very happy i took this course because it helped me make lots of pieces in my portfolio that im proud of  i wouldnt of been able to do it without professor hybinettes projects and feedback.
reviews.ratingTags: Amazing lectures --Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: The exams were the only extremely difficult thing in this class, although the projects were not very clear cut when it came to the instructions she was a very lenient grader. Her lectures weren't enjoyable, but she does care about her students and will extend any assignment at any given time.

Dr. Hybinette expects a lot; the class is fairly hard, but not as hard as these comments make it seem. Biggest thing is making sure you do well on projects. She's funny, and she does care about students.

They might be the most unorganized professor I will ever have. There have never been clear instructions for an assignment, and every assignment has been changed after it has already been released, sometimes two days before it is due. Exams have content that is not covered in class or any provided material and she constantly contradicts herself.
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Worst professor I've ever had. Unclear instructions; contradictions in examples, question-responses, & in ppts; exam questions not pulled from taught content; couldn't answer her own exam questions; was horrible w/ questions both IRL & online; and called out a student in the middle of class, who explained the content better than she could. (+ more)

Lectures were unorganized, and many of her examples had errors. Homework would be posted at the most inconvenient times, and had difficult-to-understand instructions that required constant updating. Grading is slow, and refused to curve exams with low averages. She's very kind and tries to be helpful in person, but otherwise a bad lecturer.

May be the worst professor I have had at UGA. Her lectures are borderline useless with no clear outline and a very disorganized, rambling teaching-style. Her students would correct her many times. Outside of this, her projects were awful and many times instructions were changed days before due. Tests have a failing average and she refuses a curve.
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Worst instructions ever. Homeworks are unclear and the guidelines are being constantly updated making it hard to be concise and manage your time. Unnecessarily complicated for a datastructures course and homework is 60%, requiring more time than needed. Exams are ridiculously formatted and her lectures/ppts dont help that much.
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Absolutely horrible. The assignment code is broken and never works and requirements for assignments are unclear and updated hours before due dates. Lectures are horrible and uninformative. Laughs at herself the whole time. Extremely unorganized. Tests do not relate to lectures and test material is not made clear. Unbelievably slow grading.
reviews.ratingTags: Tough grader--Lots of homework--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: This class was an absolute nightmare from start to finish. Assignments were poorly designed and did not align with the material covered in class. Grading was slow and inconsistent, with super vague feedback.

This was her first time lecturing this class. Very disorganized and many of the assignments did not have clear instructions. Many errors in the skeleton code she provides too, She's nice but most of her lectures consist of yapping.

Very kind and helpful when you see her one-on-one, but otherwise disastrously unorganized and the lectures are not helpful. Make sure you get on good terms with her personally if you want to do well in the class.

Kind professor, but did not learn anything from lecture. Great course if you can self study, she gives extra credit and only has one midterm worth 10% of grade. Doing the projects and homework took forever though (20-30 hours each), probably because I had to self study along the way and its C.
reviews.ratingTags: EXTRA CREDIT--Lots of homework--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Honestly a great professor- the best one at UGA so far. If you're willing to work, you'll learn much about CS and do well in class. She is super nice, available, and enthusiastic. Her assignments and labs are practical.  3 labs are dropped.   I highly recommend her.
reviews.ratingTags: Amazing lectures --Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback

reviews.comment: Great prof. Labs are straightforward, the projects are practical, and she makes them interesting.  Slides are helpful. She gives quick answers on the piazza and helps with coding after class, and she is really nice. The workload is heavy, but honestly, the class is enjoyable.

Incredibly nice and knowledgeable person but awful and unorganized professor! Nothing in the class was proofread and assignments could change on the fly, she's also late to class more than once. Doing well in class meant being able to decipher her instructions, which might as well be written in cuneiform. Do not take unless you already know Python!

Professor Hybinette is a good professor. For this course I will say there is a bit of a workload with homework and labs. However Dr. Hybinette offers a lot of assistance and allows you to drop 3 of the labs. Mandatory attendance can be a bit annoying but at least it gives you extra credit.

I cannot emphasize how bad this professor is. She might sound caring and sweet, but is extremely disorganized. Her lectures are good for nothing, and is hard to keep up with her pace. She cracks poor jokes and laughs at them half the time. Her TAs are unhelpful too. They WOULD NOT help you during the labs with anything, like literally anything.

In all honesty I wouldn't recommend taking Professor Hybinette. She's a very nice lady, but her teaching style is subpar and she is very disorganized. Labs every week, and HWs every few, but HWs will take you a very long time. Attendance is mandatory as well, which sucks because her lectures aren't helpful.

Lectures are pointless, labs are given to TA (who was incredibly unhelpful) hours before class. Typos on EVERY assignment, unclear instructions on cobweb link, so make sure to check random Piazza threads 12 hours before due date for random requirements. Homework is unfairly difficult for material covered, and are worth as much as exam. AVOID!

Extremely very incredibly disorganized. It feels like she did not take the time to proofread any assignments before giving them. Does not use elc and you a left searching through cobweb manually putting in urls to find any assignments.

Labs every week, HW every few weeks. Our HW kept getting pushed back so we had way less than what we were originally supposed to. This can either be a good or bad thing depending on how well you did on the HW because HW is worth 50%. Overall not a bad prof. I bombed the midterm but am still doing fine since I'm good at the labs and HW.

I found myself getting lost in every lecture. The powerpoints weren't all that helpful either. However, she grades lenietly on labs and projects.If you're looking to learn a lot without having to feel SUPER confused, I would choose another professor.

Do not take her class, I transferred into UGA from GSU compsci is done very differently over there. She may have an idea of what shes talking about but you won't know because she has poor communication skills attributed to her disorganization. Lectures are boring and goes very fast. Very unhelpful. I would find someone else to teach you if possible

Dr. Hybinette is a very smart & fun prof who makes you work for your grade. If you have the time and effort to put in, you'll learn a lot & get an A. NOTE: This is a senior-level course and Hybinette assumes you know what you're doing and can handle yourself. But honestly I had lots of fun in this class! Would recommend if you're interested in 3D!
Did not have any sort of preparation for coding whatsoever. I had a difficult time trying to figure things out by myself, and she didn't provide any guidelines at all.

Computational investment (CSCI 4170) is an interesting class but the way this class was organized ruined everything. The instructor often drifted away from what she was teaching. She expected the students to have had all the prior knowledge for the course. She is friendly with her but not good at clearing concepts and assignments were often hard.

I would advise against taking this professor. Dr. Hybinette has an incredibly disorganized way of carrying out the course. Additional requirements for projects at last minute, unclear instructions for homework, etc. You will be teaching yourself the material outside of class. Period. Kind person, not great teacher.

Hardest class I'd taken. Excruciating to be forced outside my comfort zone, but Id finally expanded beyond what I thought I could do, making this class worthwhile. You can't drift through it. Hybinettes class is only good for your grade if you put out your best effort - but you HAVE to be willing to do exactly that: Your best, or beyond.

She was incredibly disorganized- but funny and caring. It was during the pandemic, so I guess it's hard for everyone. The class was overly complicated. Definitely as hard as 1302 which it should not have been. She is way more helpful to debug after class / in private msgs. If you put forth efforts, you'll be rewarded in the end.

Prof, Hybinettes class is amazing  fun and enjoyable lectures. She provides small workable software modules (tutorials) that pieces well together for her projects (and future). Her projects are real world practical exercise. Her course is not just a checkpoint filler for my degree but will have an impact long term. Recommended course and Prof.

1730 is one of the most challenging classes at UGA. Dr. Hybinette's version of 1730 has been great, she is a great lecturer & she is fun! She is easily my favorite UGA professor I've had. I'm glad I was able to experience this class with my classmates and Professor that I did because it has been a truly real amazing time.
reviews.ratingTags: Accessible outside class--Amazing lectures--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Dr. Hybinette is a very good person (both kind & knowledgeable) but an atrocious teacher. This class was incredibly disorganized, and lectures were basically pointless (seriously, she makes content seem harder than it is- just teach yourself). To top it all off, Dr. Hybinette often changes requirements for assignments and projects very last minute.

To be frank, Dr. Hybinette does have a bit of a sink or swim mentality with the coursework. She doesn't really "teach" the content without you having to work for it yourself, but at the same time, she doesn't have strict expectations and is super nice. Those who are complaining clearly didn't put in the effort or work especially this semester.
reviews.ratingTags: Gives good feedback--Respected--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Dr. Hybinette is a great professor. She's super funny and knows her stuff when it comes to systems programming. She is always happy to help you debug code and answer any and all questions you have. She's also very responsive to student needs, and is super supportive and helpful. Amazing person/professor. Pretty easy A if you put in the work.

Dr. Hybinette is a great teacher. She is really helpful and if you ask her questions she gives really good feedback and guidance. The assignments aren't too hard you just have to do the work. Definitely take her if you actually want to learn and be prepared for the real world.

I hated this class. The professor was just such a bad teacher! The content wasn't hard, but you have to learn it all yourself. She assigned a project in C++ and gave us two weeks to do it, but the kicker is: she didn't start teaching any C++ until after the project was assigned! I heard she was a good teacher 10 years ago but now she is awful.

Hybinette is funny and has a great personality! The class however is unorganized and very stressful. Lectures could not matter less and the projects have unclear guidelines that are constantly changing VIA PIAZZA. Be ready to make massive changes to your project the night that it's due because you just found out via piazza another requirement.
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: She's sweet but a TERRIBLE teacher. I honestly didn't listen to a single lecture. Very chaotic and all over the place, no one could follow her. For the projs, the labs helped give you an idea but I just googled everything. Her requirements for projs were all over the place and confusing, she changes them/adds stuff the DAY before it's due.

Honestly a nice lady but this class so mentally draining due to how much work it was and the fact project descriptions would change on the regular didn't help. The class was GRUELING get ready for A LOT of coding and googling. It was just so mentally exhausting. I just plainly did not enjoy. Your classmates are your lifeline better make friends.

This is by far the most chaotic class I have taken at UGA, the lectures are useless and you're better off using the powerpoints and googling things yourself. The work is decently easy if you've had a couple of years of CS experience. She has changed project descriptions 3 days before due dates. She's a nice lady but an awful professor would avoid.

I enjoy coding, enjoyed the assignments, and thought Dr. Hybinette was a decent lecturer. Why the bad score? Because labs are UNFORGIVING. They're graded by checkpoints. Checkpoint 1 MUST BE COMPLETED IN CLASS IN 50 MINUTES OR ELSE YOU LOSE UP TO 50 POINTS! Even if you submit the other checkpoints on time! We're STUDENTS! Not Hackathon speedsters!
reviews.ratingTags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--Tough grader

reviews.comment: This professor is really sweet and nice but so so bad at teaching. No organization. Unclear lectures. Hard projects. I have never taken a worse class at UGA. AVOID AT ALL COSTS.

Dr. Hybinette is a wonderful teacher. The classes she teaches, however, are really dense topics that require self-study and patience. If you take her classes, be sure to engage in the lectures and start your projects early, or else you won't do well. She's a fairly lenient grader so as long as your effort comes through, you should be okay.

Dr. Hybinette is not for cry-babies, that is for sure. Just got through a brutal job interview and got the job. Dr Hybinettes class prepared me. Who needs lectures when you can do hackathons. Stop crying and do the work. Best professor I ever had. ++++

Get ready to read, because you have to teach yourself. Google will become your best friend and your classmates are the lifeboat in this sea of agony. Beware the rubric, for it changes. I suggest getting started on learning Unity now so that you are not cramming it in 2 to 3 weeks.

Hybinette rarely taught. Students pick a Monday to present and then every Monday, wed hear about what our peers are interested in. Thursdays are test/hackathon/project days where you either know it or you dont. You just take it back home and cry. We watched 2 movies in the class. The few Tuesdays where she did teach, its textbook stuff.

Prof. Hybinette is top notch, best professor at UGA. She is extremely clear, her program is well planned out and teaches you to become the viable game programmer & to think on your feet.  Her course is not for the faint hearted and the workload is heavy, but I honestly, I came out as a stronger & and more confident programmer.

Prof. Hybinette taught a challenging course that forced us to do a lot of programming. I liked how the course was organized so that we built upon what we did earlier: The last two projects centered on designing a game, and implementing our design. I strongly disagree with the reviews that requirements changed. It's just not true.
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Participation matters--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: Seeing as how my peers already made a point about her lack of organization and time management, Ive come to say that shes made a lot of changes to this class. Originally, everything was graded much harsher and had even poorer timing. If she continues on this track of taking our evals into consideration, youd be fine taking her 8 years from now.
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Grades sometimes took weeks to be returned and rubrics were unclear and sometimes even changed days before the assignment was due. The part of the game programming class where we used an actual engine was self-taught and we only had two weeks to learn the engine, a new programming language, and complete two projects (one of which was the final).
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Tough grader

reviews.comment: Instructor showed up late to class consistently. Many rubrics were vague and self contradicting. She also tried to change final exam rubric 2 days prior to end of class. Provided outdated information about Unity then expected us to become proficient on an entire textbook on game dev. in 2 weeks. Class constantly behind schedule.
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read--Tough grader

reviews.comment: She clearly cannot properly organise a class. I felt constantly stressed through the entire course and have learned nothing in return. Many homeworks and projects felt pointless and out of place. Also she constantly changes the grading rubrics, so it's hard to understand what she is expecting in projects.
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Get ready to read

reviews.comment: The pacing of her game dev class is ridiculous, you spend about 3/4 of the semester learning how to do useless things. And then with 3 weeks left she drops 2 massive projects based around Unity which she DOES NOT teach. She gives you an out of date textbook and then you're on your own to make a full 3D game in 6 days DURING FINALS.

Dr. Hybinette's OS class has been the most difficult class I have ever taken in college. 1/3rd of the class dropped and the rest of us live in agony. This class has pushed my mental health to the brink, but I will say I have learned a significant amount and her grading has honestly been very fair. The workload is brutal and shes a bit brash. 6.5/10

Dr. Hybinette is a really tough teacher. Her lectures aren't the best, but her projects help you learn A LOT! While they are difficult, they are fun and rewarding. Taking any of her classes requires a lot of effort, but you will learn so much. Highly recommended for those looking to learn.

The amount of coursework in this class is mind-boggling. We did a project every 2 weeks, which all ended up being extended due to difficulty. 2 projects expected us to learn Python from scratch. Also, a group presents an academic paper every week and you have to read and make a summary on in by midnight on THE SAME DAY. Grading scale is not clear.

Dr H is amazing!  Her class is fun and challenging.  Projectsand "Hackathons" make you think and challenge you to program (quickly).   Regarding the hackathons: At first I did not like the weekly hackathons, but now I realize I am becoming a great programmer because of them.

The professor is nice but BLOWS at teaching. Stupid labs (aka: hackathons) that don't help you learn and are expected to be done in the 50 min which most don't complete. Has two part exams, first part being the hackathon and the second being the exam. Projects have so many typos and are super unclear and vague. Avoid if possible.

I'm only three weeks in, and this course is hell. She expects you to learn C in a week. There are weekly labs which must be completed within the hour, or you get points docked. Nobody gets close to finishing in the hour, and when asked for help replies "This stuff is easy." Really unimpressive. I'm avoiding her classes in the future. You should too

Dr. Hybinette's classes are well worth taking. Throughout the class she is concerned about the her student's well being and grasp of the material. However, the class will take a lot of your time(Homework, projects, presentations, papers, quizzes, and tests). However, you will learn the material. Realistically it feels like 1.5 classes. Recommended!

I took programming languages with Professor Hybinette and the class was a lot of work but the most useful class I have taken in computer science.  The atmosphere in the class was great and she cares a lot about her students
Dr Hybinette was a very good teacher. She provided lots of examples that were easy to understand. Also, you could tell that she cared if we were learning or not.

Went too fast through the material; often assumed the class understood it when we were dead silent. Projects a bit difficult, and near the end were given without a lot of time to work on them. Occasional problems communicating with her in class. Curves quizzes and the final dramatically; should help make up for lost points on project grades.

Took this class for algortihms. there were 2 exams, about 6 quizzes where 4 of them were online and the in person quizzes were fairly easy, she tells what will be on them and she also tells whats on the exam as well. no real attendance was ever taken. 6 homeworks which take a lil bit of time to do but definitely doable.

I only got a B- because of my own arrogance. This is an extremely straightforward class that lays out exactly what you need to do to succeed. There's homework and quizzes that are both done online at home, and two exams in person that are easy.

For CSCI 4470 (Algorithms). Dr. Keshtgari is a great professor and very straightforward with her class. It is very black and white on what/how to study and be successful in her class. Attendance is not mandatory, and she records and posts all lectures. I would say attending class is not crucial, but doing practice problems is essential.
reviews.ratingTags: Clear grading criteria--Gives good feedback--Lots of homework

reviews.comment: I really enjoyed this professor. She posted majority of her lectures videos and slides online. Quizzes are similar to the homeworks given. For midterm and final she gives you everything you should(and should not) study for so you do not study unnecessary material. Course work is manageable and very doable.
Very solid professor. Sit in the front of the class if you can, can be hard to hear sometimes. Super nice and willing to help / answer questions, lectures can be a bit confusing at times, though.
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Professor Keshtgari is one of my favorite professors. I took her class for 2610 as well. She is clear and transparent about expectations for the class as well as what content will be on quizzes/exams. Quizzes are similar to hw and you are allowed a one-side cheat sheet for every quiz and exam. If you take the homeworks seriously you will do good.

Took her for online architecture and she was fantastic. Her videos are just recordings of her actual lectures and she provides really good answer key videos after quizzes/homeworks are due.
