She also allows a one page cheat sheet, 95 for an A is a bit crazy but it is what it is. She has her online class down.

She always answers my emails and is very kind. Although the grading is rough, it's achievable. The content itself is straightforward and having a cheatsheet makes it even easier. Before the test, she will give specific learning targets for each question that will be on the test. I bombed a couple of quizzes and HWs, yet ended with an A somehow.

Overall easy class, All the notes, lecture videos and quizzes are online so pretty easy class to self study. Just the midterm and final are in-person and you need a 95+ for an A so be aware. Really caring professor and always available. Would recommend to take her.

Dr. Keshtgari is amazing & very clear about what is expected. Need to get 95 for A+ but if you study up it's very doable. Pay attention to MIPS when she first teaches about loops in assembly bc it will keep coming back. Quizzes & exams are based on lecture & she gives lots of example questions if you attend.

Dr. Keshtgari is an overall solid professor. She's a nice lady, and the exams are easy relative to the class. My only gripe is that 95 is an A, and she is very strict on no rounding. Attendance is not mandatory, and she posts review videos for almost every topic. Thus, missing a class or two is not a big deal. Quizzes and home are done online.

Great lecturer, can be boring, but is straightforward and has lesson videos online if you miss class. Very sweet and understanding if you have extenuating circumstances. Grade based only on hw, quizzes, midterm, and final, and 95+ to get an A, but very easy to do well if you study the lecture material and practice. And 1 hw and 1 quiz get dropped.
reviews.ratingTags: Clear grading criteria--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Absolutely no complaints whatsoever. I showed up to class maybe four times and accessed the material online. Very easy homework and quizzes due every couple weeks or so. The midterm and final and very straightforward and simple.

Dr. Keshtgari was very helpful when any questions were asked. The class itself is straightforward in the material. There are 6 homeworks, 6 quizzes, and 2 exams as the grades in this class. The lowest homework and quiz gets dropped after the last of both are completed and the lectures are very helpful. If you do the homework, you will do fine.
reviews.ratingTags: Gives good feedback--Caring--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: I would maybe take off a half point for the fact that she makes an A start at 95% instead of the usual 93%, but other than that the class with her was great. Most of the necessary materials to succeed on quizzes and tests are posted online as slides and videos anyway, but coming to class will definitely help your understanding.

The threshold for an A is 95 or above which worried me at first but definitely doable. For the midterm and final she goes through and lists exactly what topic each question will be. Both were super easy and make up 55% of your grade. Super nice lady, easy class. She wants you to do well!

Pretty good lecturer, accent wasn't a problem at all for me. Overall, this is an easy required course so enjoy that while it lasts, but that is to say still treat the course seriously, most concepts are easy but make sure you fully understand them. Margin for an A is very small, at a 95.00+ no rounding. 6 HWs, 6 Quizzes, 1 Midterm, 1 Final.
reviews.ratingTags: Clear grading criteria--Test heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Smart person and good at teaching but a bit unreasonable and inflexible. Thankfully, discrete mathematics is an easy course, but be advised that she has no makeups under any circumstances (her words) and the cutoff to make an A is a 95.

95 for A, but lenient grader and more or less tells you exactly what will be on quizzes/tests. Use hw to study and focus on what she tells you to. Content isn't too hard and workload isnt too bad. Use quizzes to study for exams as well. Straightforward class and she wants you to do well. Attendance is helpful for examples/hearing abt quiz/exam.

Attendance isn't mandatory but it is good for practice problems. Graded by quizzes, homework, midterm and final. She posts lectures online so if you miss a class or need a review it is there. For the quizzes, midterm, and final you get a cheat sheet that you can use. Overall need to put in time to understand the material but a doable class.
reviews.ratingTags: Clear grading criteria--Lecture heavy--Graded by few things

reviews.comment: Homework is somewhat similar to content on quizzes. To make an A you need a 95 or above which was pretty awful, but it is doable. Review in-class problems, online materials, and HW to be best prepared for assessments.

While lectures werent the easiest to understand she explained the concepts whenever asked and give good examples to explain them. You are allowed a cheat sheet on the tests and quizzes. Tests were much easier than quizzes.

Attendance wasn't mandatory. Only 2 exams in the whole class but a bunch of quizzes and tests. Professor taught well but was a little hard to understand at times. Videos of lectures and slides are available online. Quizzes are at least as or (almost always) harder than the exams as long as you've done your homework. Formula sheet allowed.

Starting out in this class I didn't interact with it or anything, but then I saw my grade and was worried about my career. I then started getting tutoring and going to classes, and realized that her class wasn't difficult in the slightest you just have to do a little bit of work. Harder at the start of the semester than the end. Great professor.

If you can memorize everything she says in her lectures (absorb it ALL), you'll do well. Questions on assessments are specific to these, but they aren't difficult. She doesn't try to trick you, but you have to absorb every single thing she says because she will pull from something she went over for like 30 seconds in a video. Straightforward though

Dr. Keshtgari was great! Although she only counts A's as 95 or above, she makes it really easy to achieve. The quizzes often have questions we went over in class. I missed my final for this class because I mixed up the dates, but she was so understanding that I was able to reschedule it despite my mistake.
The most goated professor on the planet. Her tests are super easy if you study the homework and the quiz solutions. Never came to class because all of her videos are online. 10/10 the best teacher.

Most quizzes out of class. One page reference sheet allowed. Prerecorded lectures offer useful review. In-class examples often directly reference hw questions and quiz questions. Keshtgari helped me after lecture when I had lingering questions. Hardly anything to complain about except for the guy who cheated next to me on final exam.

I showed up to this class a total of like two times, never learned any of the stuff until the day before the midterm/final (which make up 50% of the grade) and got 100's on both. The only reason I didn't end with an A is because the quizzes were actually pretty tough, but if you just do the homework and do the quizzes this class is a joke.

Was one of the best lecturers I had this semester. She cares about her students a lot. If you study and put in the time for this class you will be fine she got your back. Even though an A is 95+, if you put in the work she will make sure you get an A. Sometimes, if you submit a homework without material fully learned she will let you resubmit.

Dr. Keshtagari truly cares about her students and wants everyone to pass. Her idea is that she makes difficult in-class examples and homework questions, to make easier questions on quizzes. Midterm is easy if you review past quizzes! I highly recommend going to her lectures, she gives out homework answers & often uses in-class examples on quizzes.

You can do well without showing up to class if you watch her lecture videos, but she gives a lot of prep and sometimes homework answers in class. She gives the topics of the midterms and finals and a really good review beforehand. She won't round the final grade! Ex: 94.9999 is still a A-. Also beware the TAs, I had to bargain for points with mine
She's good only by comparison to the atrocious teaching abilities of other CS professors. She can't explain beyond what's on the slides. Offers extra credit though.

She is one of my favorite professors in the CS department. Her lectures are engaging and she is knowledgable about the content she is talking about. The one downside to the class is you need to make a 95 for an A in the class; however, she offers so many opportunities for extra credit if your participate in class.

Eh, an ok prof. She just rambles throughout class and doesn't care about her students. The class is easy tho, most students didn't even show up and did well. She does not respond to emails well and you'll probably self-study this class. Just a mediocre professor, but a sweet lady with no passion in the lecture hall. All in all, take her if you can.

Professor Keshtgari is very knowledgeable about the subject. Sometimes she can be a bit confusing and makes concepts way harder than they should be, but she is always willing to explain it again. She offers extra credit as well. This is a tough class, but it gets easier towards the end.
it is a 95 to get an a, but not that bad if you study for the quizzes and study a lot for midterm and final. she does do a dropped quiz and hw. programming assignments are pretty eas

Attendance is not mandatory. Posts videos online. One page cheat sheet for quizzes/midterm/final. Homeworks can be annoying at times. She is a very sweet person but its easy to get distracted in her class. If you can get another professor that has a higher rating then do so. But she is not a bad choice for this class at all.
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Test heavy--Accessible outside class

reviews.comment: Honestly not great. Not the best lecturer, just really boring. Quizzes were really hard. She's really nice, but also has a very tough grading scale. You need a 95+ to get an A.

Professor Keshtgari made sure that we understood the material we were being taught. We were given in-class examples, videos, and slides to prepare for homework and quizzes. If she is teaching any CS class, try to get her as your professor.
Dr. Keshtgari is the most organized professor I have had. The class overall is not bad and the professor is good.
It is a 95 to get an A in a rigours upper level CS course ...

Keshtgari is very kind and truly cares about her students. Quiz and homework dates had to be changed sometimes, but she was very flexible and lenient. The course is difficult, but she does her best to make it accessible. One of the few professors here who values actual learning over a letter grade.
Overall great professor with easy and manageable material during the Summer, really wants you to learn, but the content near the end gets confusing.

brutal amount of work over the summer especially if its not your only class. just make sure you watch the lecture videos as soon as they're unlocked and start projects way earlier than you think you need to

Dr. Keshtgari is such a sweet professor! She always responds to emails in a timely manner. Even though the course schedule kept changing, she was good about communicating everything to us. She also offers extra credit on HW, quizzes, and the final. She even helped us on the final exam if we had questions and would point us in the right direction.

She's nice, but nice does not mean that they are a good professor. She teaches in-person but same as her online videos. Her lectures are very boring to listen. No extra credit for anything beside hw/quiz. Final is in-person. Study Guide was a doc with every unit listed. HW/Quiz was very inconvenient, most time was spent uploading pictures of work

Dr. Keshtgari is a very knowledgeable professor and cares about her students. Her lectures were pretty helpful and a good measure of what would be on hw/exams. She was quick to answer questions via email and was understanding and lenient with deadlines. Final was in person, but she gave hints during the exam to steer us in the right direction.

Dr. Keshtgari is one of the sweetest professors I have ever had. Although she constantly changes the course schedule, the workload isn't too bad. She is quick with responding to emails, and during class she always answers questions. Even during the final exam, she would make sure I was on the right track if I asked her to check my work.

Had her for networks. This is an extremely information-heavy class, but if you pay attention in class it shouldn't be too difficult. She doesn't check for attendance but if you don't go you will get lost. She's very knowledgeable on the topic and always answers questions clearly. 3 small programming assignments, 6 hw/quizzes, & 2 exams in total.
reviews.ratingTags: Get ready to read--Gives good feedback--Lecture heavy

reviews.comment: Don't be misled by the other ratings. Extremely hard to understand through a very strong accent. Unbelievably bad at explaining the most simple concepts. Very annoying and unintuitive quizzes. The only reason she doesn't get a 1 is she didn't have a textbook or necessary attendance.

Not sure why her reviews are so high. She is very hard to understand and her answers are often wrong and completely misguiding. She had the wrong answer to a question on her quiz and refused to acknowledge that her answer was not correct.

The professor is very sweet and helps you understand the material. The course itself is very rigorous and can be tough on a beginner. There are so many assignments like labs and projects you have to keep up with.

extremely nice professor, was always willing to extend homeworks/quizzes as needed and will always be open to take questions. the work for the class can get very confusing at times but she tries to make it as easy as she can. grading was also very lenient on exams

Not sure why she has high reviews for 4720. They say she's organized but I don't agree. No guidance for learning MIPS other than handwritten notes posted as PDFs that are barely legible and badly formatted. Need outside sources to learn MIPS. Online quizzes are brutal, you have to upload hand-drawn answers. Chegg is required to finish in time.

Probably the best organized class I've taken. Very straightforward, 6 modules, one homework and quiz per module, 4 programming assignments total. Attendance is not required but homeworks, quizzes, programming assignments, and tests are almost directly from lectures. Lectures are very meticulous and Dr. Keshtgari is happy to answer questions.

Very knowledgeable in the subject material. She likes to go through example problems in class that will be on homework, quizzes, and the tests. Make sure to follow along with her so you understand how to do the problems.
reviews.ratingTags: Accessible outside class--Skip class? You won't pass.--Caring

reviews.comment: Dr. Keshtgari is really kind. She is very helpful one on one. Her quizzes and tests are similar to the homework she gives. If you answer homework questions during class, she will give you bonus points. One of my favorite professors at UGA.

Keshtgari is a great professor. However, I really had a tough time absorbing her lectures. The homework and quiz problems weren't hard to figure out (basic formulas for most things) but the concepts were hard to grasp. I had her in the past for another class where she explained things better, so I think this was due to online/Zoom lectures.

I took networks class with her and it was awful. Her lectures are monotonic which does not help stay engaged. Her projects and test are aligned with lectures so you'll be learning a lot of it from the internet. If another option is available, don't take her.
reviews.ratingTags: Skip class? You won't pass.--Lecture heavy--Tough grader

reviews.comment: I am so grateful for Keshtgari during the trying times we are currently in. She continuously was extremely understanding and caring with our deadlines and adjusting to the pandemic. Reading the textbook really helps, the lectures are pre-recorded and the amount of homework was a good amount. There was always enough time & she communicates well!

Professor Keshtgari was one of the few professors I had this semester that took into account how this pandemic was affecting us students. She was super understanding, easy to contact, and noticeably put much time and effort into her lectures. She is an extremely kind and respects her students. I am very grateful I took her class.

Take her if you can. Seriously, she's that good. She adapted to corona well; she uploads videos covering lectures so you didn't have to attend class. The class was organized well too. And she's very understanding, so reach out to her through email if you have any problems, she's very nice and helpful. You do have to put in work though.

Took Networks during covid, halfway through the semester she required a lockdown browser for quizzes and tests even though the average on the midterm was an 80 before. There is way too much information to memorize from the short "lecture" videos she posts, and sometimes the quiz questions arent even from the lecture. Made a hard class way harder

as long as you keep up with lecture everything else is really easy. the tests are a little harder than the quizzes the quizzes are a little harder than the homework. overall, it's very manageable to make an A in the class. super sweet lady

If I could take all my CS classes with her I think I would. The lectures are so boring but the grading for all her classes is the same (this is my third with her). Homeworks (that are easy), followed by quizzes the day after you go over homework (quiz based off of HW) and tests where she tells you the number and specific context of each question.

Shes wonderful and genuinely cares about her students understanding and passing her class. She gets excited to see students get As. Its best to come to class, although its optional, since she explains much better than the textbook assigned. In fact, getting a textbook for her class is useless unless you dont come to class.

reviews.comment: This class was very challenging but she is honestly the best teacher to have for it. She gives a lot of extra credit and is so helpful in office hours. Her tests and quizzes are modeled just like her homeworks, so you have a good idea of what you'll be tested on. I would definitely recommend taking this class with her!

reviews.comment: She is a great teacher. Honestly, if you pay attention in class and go over powerpoints you will be able to do hws, quizzes, and tests. Quizzes- go over notes and powerpoints not just HW!! Gives +5 if you answer hw/quiz answers the next class. She is very helped in office hours if you're ever confused, wish I had gone earlier. Also sweetest lady!!

This class was pretty tough. She does a good job at explaining material although at times the concepts can be hard to follow. Try to do well at paying attention in class and make sure to do the homework and study for quizzes to prepare for the tests.
She is so great! You have to really pay attention when she is teaching to grasp concepts. She gives many extra credit opportunities, so participate!

I love Keshtgari!! She's so helpful and answers your questions the best she can, but sometimes she doesn't understand it at first but it's okay just explain it again! Super helpful and extremely smart I wish I could have her for all my 4000-level classes
This teacher is like your sweet grandmother. If your sweet grandmother was way smarter than you'll ever be and could use a computer.

The midterm and final also count for a lot lot but they are not particularly hard either. Textbook was occasionally helpful, but not mandatory at all.

Homeworks assigned every other week or so and a quiz after every homework. Very straightforward and interesting class. But the best part is she's an absolute peach and overall just a cutie!!

class was super easy, 6 homeworks, a few quizzes, and 2 tests. the concepts arent hard and the powerpoints have all the info you need. going to class was not necessary. ta's werent helpful but she is. she's nice and wants everyone to pass but sometimes has difficulty explaining things. overall easy class with not a lot of work
